Title: Data Specialist para Riesgo de Crédito / Pricing
Description:
Somos Banco Sabadell




Banco Sabadell es una de las principales entidades bancarias en España con más de 14.000 personas caracterizadas por nuestra implicación, espíritu de colaboración y dinamismo. Banco Sabadell ofrece sus servicios a más de 11 millones de clientes contando con una posición privilegiada en el segmento de empresas.




Nuestra razón de ser es ayudar a personas y empresas a hacer realidad sus proyectos, anticipándonos y ocupándonos de que tomen las mejores decisiones económicas, a través de nuestros valores: el compromiso, no conformismo, profesionalidad, eficacia, empatía y franqueza.

Y además, lo hacemos mediante una gestión responsable y comprometida con el medio ambiente.




¡Únete a nosotros!




¿Qué estamos buscando?




Actualmente buscamos un perfil con visión transversal y dinámico, con capacidad analítica y de síntesis, que tenga interés por aprender y desarrollar nuevas funciones y actividades relativas a la formación y seguimiento del precio de las operaciones y, en general, de riesgo de crédito.




Hablemos del proyecto…




Tu misión principal se centrará en velar por que los precios de las operaciones de activo recojan adecuadamente los riesgos asumidos.




Entre tus responsabilidades principales se encontrarán:




Analizarás los componentes vinculados al pricing para las diferentes carteras.
Análizarás las principales operaciones de riesgo concedidas, calculando su rentabilidad ajustada a los riesgos asumidos y reportando los cálculos realizados a los negocios.
Desarrollarás sistemas de control de la rentabilidad ajustada al riesgo de todas las operaciones concedidas y revisarás las hipótesis de valoración.
Harás seguimiento de la evolución de los precios de nueva entrada de activo y pasivo del Banco.
Desarrollarás y harás mantenimiento de herramientas de cálculo de pricing para las distintas carteras del Banco.
Confeccionarás informes para los diferentes Comités / Comisiones sobre la evolución de los precios respecto a los riesgos asumidos en las operaciones, elaborando análisis específicos de rentabilidad de carteras / clientes / operaciones que incorporen la visión de riesgo (interés, crédito, liquidez, concentración).
Participarás en el desarrollo de la estrategia de riesgo de crédito del Grupo, y elaborarás y analizarás información sobre benchmarks sectoriales, expectativas supervisoras, presentación a inversores, etc.




¿Qué valoramos de tu candidatura?




Que tengas estudios superiores en ámbito Técnico (Matemáticas, Estadística, Ingeniería, Física o similar) o Empresarial (Economía, ADE o similar), con orientación cuantitativa.
Que aportes un mínimo de 2-3 años de experiencia en posición similar en Banca, con experiencia contrastada en análisis masivo de datos y en el uso de herramientas que permiten su tratamiento de manera automatizada (SAS, lenguaje de programación SQL o similar).
Valoraremos positivamente conocimientos y/o experiencia en el cálculo de rentabilidades de operaciones / carteras.
Que seas usuario avanzado del paquete Office (especialmente Excel y PowerPoint).
Que aportes un correcto nivel de Inglés (B2).







Banco Sabadell está comprometido en promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, procurando el desarrollo profesional de la plantilla y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, orientación sexual, religión, etnia o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social.




Banco Sabadell forma parte de la red de empresas con el distintivo de “Igualdad en la Empresa” otorgado por el Ministerio de Igualdad.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Tareas



Análisis del uso que hace el cliente de los datos y estratégia de negocio.


Detección de oportunidades de explotación de sus datos y de datos externos con alto impacto en su negocio.


Exploración y análisis de datos, identificación de variables relevantes para resolver retos concretos.


Estudio y modelización de la estructura global de datos del cliente.


Construcción de soluciones a problemas de negocio en base a datos de principio a fin (compresión de necesidad, integrción del dato, curación de datos, visualización con herramientas de business intelligence, construcción de algoritmos y modelos predictivos).


Comunicación de resultados e interacción directa con clientes.



Requisitos



Titulación: Grado en Matemáticas, Física, Ingeniería, Química. Máster en BI, Business Analytics, Data Science, o experiencia equivalente.


Herramientas: SQL, Python


Nivel de Español hablado y escrito nativo.


Sensibilidad de negocio y pensamiento estratégico.


Fuertes habilidades analíticas


Capacidad de comunicación y presentación de resultados analíticos



Beneficios



Participar en proyectos innovadores en el ámbito del Data Science (nuestra tecnología combina BI con soluciones de machine learning adaptadas a cada cliente y con las que él mismo interacciona).


Contacto directo con los clientes y acceso a problemas reales a los que se enfrentan las empresas, diseñando soluciones con un alto impacto.


Formar parte de una startup en crecimiento y colaborar a diario con un equipo de expertos en consultoría estrategia, data science y desarrollo de software en la nube.


Trabajar con tecnologías de última generación





Beneficios Prenomics





Horario de trabajo flexible e híbrido


Suministro ilimitado de bebidas con y sin cafeína


Horas semanales dedicadas a apreder y discutir nuevas tecnologías


Sistema de retribución flexible: tickets restaurante y transporte


Evaluaciones de desempeño, diseño de plan de carrera y revisiones salariales anuales


Actividades de teambuilding con todo el equipo

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Who we are:
At Rover, pets and their people are at the heart of everything we do. We connect pet parents with trusted pet care across the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Australia.

Headquartered in Seattle and Barcelona, we’re a values driven, fast-growing tech company focused on building safe and personalized experiences tailored to the needs of each unique pet. We’re investing in AI as a business accelerator and provide every team member access to AI tools in service of creating better experiences for our community.

We are proud to be recognized as a great place to work, having been named among the 100 Best Companies to Work For in Seattle Business Magazine and Washington’s Best Workplaces in the Puget Sound Business Journal. At Rover we’re committed to creating an accessible, inclusive, and welcoming community, which starts with our employees.

Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.

Who we're looking for

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our Barcelona Marketing Analytics team, partnering closely with Lifecycle Marketing (our CRM / retention function) to shape how Rover measures and grows long-term customer value.

The Barcelona team's north star is simple: drive more repeat bookings and lengthen LTV across both pet parents and sitters. Everything we do, from email and push experimentation to attribution work to measurement framework design, ladders up to that.

You'll own meaningful pieces of that work as an independent contributor. You'll be the person who turns messy marketing data into reporting that leadership can actually lean on; who partners with our Lifecycle Marketing, Martech, and Analytics Engineering teams to QA business logic and keep our data clean; and who designs and analyzes tests, not only classic A/B tests, but cross-channel retargeting and BD experiments where the measurement gets harder. When our holdout strategy hits its limits, you'll be part of the team figuring out what comes next.

This role rewards intellectual curiosity. Our incrementality analyses wrestle with real puzzles: mix-shift between markets, novelty-effect decay on mature cohorts, holdout leakage, and interaction between transactional and marketing sends. We don't expect you to have solved all of these before—we expect you to be genuinely excited to dig in.

And because Rover is a two-sided marketplace, you'll hold both sides in mind. The best analyses we do connect the dots.

Your impact
Own reporting that Marketing actually trusts. Build and maintain the dashboards, data models, and reporting layers that Lifecycle Marketing and broader Marketing leadership rely on day-to-day. Proactively identify where our reporting is noisy, inconsistent, or missing, and fix it. You'll be the go-to person Marketing leads come to when a number looks off.

Design and analyze experiments. Own the end-to-end lifecycle of tests in your area—from scoping the hypothesis and picking the right measurement, to running the analysis, to writing up the result and socializing what we learned. You'll partner with Lifecycle Marketing on their monthly testing cadence, and you'll take on harder cross-channel and retargeting tests where the measurement isn't obvious.

Support the evolution of our measurement framework. You'll partner with the team on incrementality work, stratified holdouts, and attribution model refinement—bringing statistical rigor to questions like how much lift is real, how much is novelty, and how much is noise?

Be the analytical bridge to Martech. Partner with our Martech and Analytics Engineering teams to QA business logic in our CRM pipelines, validate audience data, and make sure the campaigns Lifecycle Marketing launches are measurable from day one. You'll be the analytics voice in conversations about tooling and data flow.

Unblock the team. A senior IC on this team makes everyone around them more effective. You'll review SQL, pressure-test analytical approaches, and help raise the bar on how we structure our work.

Tell stories that drive action. Root-cause analysis is core to this role: when a metric moves, you'll go find out why, tie it to marketplace dynamics on both the demand and supply side, and bring a clear narrative to the people who need to act on it.

Your Qualifications


3+ years of experience using data to drive business impact, including identifying actionable insights, crafting compelling narratives, and partnering with cross-functional teams to define and implement solutions.
Strong proficiency in SQL—confident writing complex queries against large event-based datasets, and comfortable reviewing and improving code from other analysts.
Hands-on experience building and maintaining transformation models (dbt or similar) and a point of view on what makes a good data model.
Solid grounding in statistical analysis using Python or R—hypothesis testing, regression, bootstrapping—and an understanding of the trade-offs between different measurement approaches.
Proven experience owning A/B testing end-to-end: experimental design, sample size, analysis, and write-up.
A clear communicator who can make complex analyses land with both technical and non-technical audiences, and who enjoys partnering directly with business stakeholders.
Demonstrated ability to manage analytical work independently—scoping, prioritizing, and delivering without needing to be micromanaged.
Data visualization tool experience (e.g., Mode, Looker, Tableau).
Genuine intellectual curiosity about measurement problems, and comfort sitting with ambiguity while you figure them out.




Nice to have


Experience supporting a Lifecycle Marketing, CRM, or retention function, particularly with email, push, or SMS programs.
Familiarity with marketing attribution concepts (MMM, last-touch, multi-touch) and an interest in where attribution modeling is headed.
Exposure to incrementality testing, causal impact models, geo-based experiments, or similar approaches beyond classic A/B holdouts.
Experience in a two-sided marketplace, with a gut feel for how supply and demand dynamics show up in the data.
Familiarity with any part of our stack: Amazon Redshift, dbt, Mode, Statsig, Amplitude, Iterable, or Hightouch.
Track record of mentoring junior analysts or raising the technical bar on a team without formal management responsibility.




Benefits Of Working At Rover


Long-term incentive plan with a company performance-based cash payout
Pension plan
Private medical insurance
25 days PTO
Meal allowance and flexible compensation plan (transport and nursery)
Gym membership
€450 to cover the costs associated with the adoption of a pet
Annual €150 wellness reimbursement
Flexible work hours, sometimes you'll need to be in at certain times, but on the whole, we're pretty flexible when it comes to managing workload and time
Grab snacks, fresh fruit, in our kitchen to keep yourself going
Regular team activities, events, game nights, and more
Dog-friendly office




Rover is an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting a diverse, inclusive, and inventive environment with the best employees. We’re driven by seeing our people succeed and grow, and we work to ensure everyone contributes to their fullest potential. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, protected veteran status, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances.

You'll be the person who turns messy marketing data into reporting that leadership can actually lean on; who partners with our Lifecycle Marketing, Martech, and Analytics Engineering teams to QA business logic and keep our data clean; and who designs and analyzes tests, not only classic A/B tests, but cross-channel retargeting and BD experiments where the measurement gets harder. When our holdout strategy hits its limits, you'll be part of the team figuring out what comes next.

This role rewards intellectual curiosity. Our incrementality analyses wrestle with real puzzles: mix-shift between markets, novelty-effect decay on mature cohorts, holdout leakage, and interaction between transactional and marketing sends. We don't expect you to have solved all of these before—we expect you to be genuinely excited to dig in.

And because Rover is a two-sided marketplace, you'll hold both sides in mind. The best analyses we do connect the dots.

Your impact
Own reporting that Marketing actually trusts. Build and maintain the dashboards, data models, and reporting layers that Lifecycle Marketing and broader Marketing leadership rely on day-to-day. Proactively identify where our reporting is noisy, inconsistent, or missing, and fix it. You'll be the go-to person Marketing leads come to when a number looks off.

Design and analyze experiments. Own the end-to-end lifecycle of tests in your area—from scoping the hypothesis and picking the right measurement, to running the analysis, to writing up the result and socializing what we learned. You'll partner with Lifecycle Marketing on their monthly testing cadence, and you'll take on harder cross-channel and retargeting tests where the measurement isn't obvious.

Support the evolution of our measurement framework. You'll partner with the team on incrementality work, stratified holdouts, and attribution model refinement—bringing statistical rigor to questions like how much lift is real, how much is novelty, and how much is noise?

Be the analytical bridge to Martech. Partner with our Martech and Analytics Engineering teams to QA business logic in our CRM pipelines, validate audience data, and make sure the campaigns Lifecycle Marketing launches are measurable from day one. You'll be the analytics voice in conversations about tooling and data flow.

Unblock the team. A senior IC on this team makes everyone around them more effective. You'll review SQL, pressure-test analytical approaches, and help raise the bar on how we structure our work.

Tell stories that drive action. Root-cause analysis is core to this role: when a metric moves, you'll go find out why, tie it to marketplace dynamics on both the demand and supply side, and bring a clear narrative to the people who need to act on it.

Your Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience using data to drive business impact, including identifying actionable insights, crafting compelling narratives, and partnering with cross-functional teams to define and implement solutions.
  • Strong proficiency in SQL—confident writing complex queries against large event-based datasets, and comfortable reviewing and improving code from other analysts.
  • Hands-on experience building and maintaining transformation models (dbt or similar) and a point of view on what makes a good data model.
  • Solid grounding in statistical analysis using Python or R—hypothesis testing, regression, bootstrapping—and an understanding of the trade-offs between different measurement approaches.
  • Proven experience owning A/B testing end-to-end: experimental design, sample size, analysis, and write-up.
  • A clear communicator who can make complex analyses land with both technical and non-technical audiences, and who enjoys partnering directly with business stakeholders.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage analytical work independently—scoping, prioritizing, and delivering without needing to be micromanaged.
  • Data visualization tool experience (e.g., Mode, Looker, Tableau).
  • Genuine intellectual curiosity about measurement problems, and comfort sitting with ambiguity while you figure them out.



Nice to have

  • Experience supporting a Lifecycle Marketing, CRM, or retention function, particularly with email, push, or SMS programs.
  • Familiarity with marketing attribution concepts (MMM, last-touch, multi-touch) and an interest in where attribution modeling is headed.
  • Exposure to incrementality testing, causal impact models, geo-based experiments, or similar approaches beyond classic A/B holdouts.
  • Experience in a two-sided marketplace, with a gut feel for how supply and demand dynamics show up in the data.
  • Familiarity with any part of our stack: Amazon Redshift, dbt, Mode, Statsig, Amplitude, Iterable, or Hightouch.
  • Track record of mentoring junior analysts or raising the technical bar on a team without formal management responsibility.



Benefits Of Working At Rover

  • Long-term incentive plan with a company performance-based cash payout
  • Pension plan
  • Private medical insurance
  • 25 days PTO
  • Meal allowance and flexible compensation plan (transport and nursery)
  • Gym membership
  • €450 to cover the costs associated with the adoption of a pet
  • Annual €150 wellness reimbursement
  • Flexible work hours, sometimes you'll need to be in at certain times, but on the whole, we're pretty flexible when it comes to managing workload and time
  • Grab snacks, fresh fruit, in our kitchen to keep yourself going
  • Regular team activities, events, game nights, and more
  • Dog-friendly office



Rover is an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting a diverse, inclusive, and inventive environment with the best employees. We’re driven by seeing our people succeed and grow, and we work to ensure everyone contributes to their fullest potential. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, protected veteran status, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances.

Title: Data & Business Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
Esade is a global academic institution with strong ties to the business world, renowned for the quality of its education, its international reach, and its clear focus on the comprehensive development of individuals.




Do Good Do Better is our identity, and we build it through the following values:

Integrity
Diversity
Sensitivity
Contribution to the common good
Justice and sustainability




Based on these pillars, we work with a methodology whose mission is the comprehensive education of competent and socially responsible professionals. That is why we promote inclusive leadership, academic innovation, technology, and solidarity.




If you want to be part of an organization like Esade, read the description below:




Main responsibilities:

As a Data & Business Analyst within the Esade Careers Team, you will play a key role in enabling data‑driven decision-making across the department. You will manage, analyze, and translate data related to student outcomes, internships, and employment trends into actionable insights for business stakeholders.




This role is ideal for someone who enjoys balancing independent analytical work with collaborative problem‑solving.




Data Analysis, Reporting & Presentation

Preparing ad hoc analyses and reports using Excel, Power Query, Power BI.
Identify meaningful insights beyond raw data and develop clear, concise summaries for stakeholders.
Monitor key activities and initiatives, providing actionable recommendations for continuous improvement.
Define and maintain KPIs to evaluate the effectiveness of career development programs and initiatives.
Present insights in an accessible and compelling manner to primarily non‑technical audiences




Student Outcomes & Internship Data Ownership

Support teams in data input and validation tasks.
Update recurring reports and dashboards related to student internships and full-time outcomes.
Analyze career paths and outcomes for graduates, including employment rates, job placements, compensation data, and other relevant metrics.
Run cohort analyses to understand the performance of specific programs in the job market.




Stakeholder Management & Collaboration

Collaborate closely with the team lead and all teams across Careers to understand their data needs and provide timely, high-quality support.
Adapt communication style for diverse audiences, including both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
Anticipate business needs and offer proactive data-driven insights and recommendations.
Work cross-functionally with other departments such as the Central Data Team, Quality & Accreditations, Business Enablement, Admissions, Rankings, and Programs to develop a holistic analytical perspective.
Set and communicate clear expectations, deliverables, and timelines for requests.




Request Management

Collect business requirements for on-demand analyses, reports, and dashboard enhancements.
Guide stakeholders in clarifying needs, structuring questions, and defining the right analytical approach.
Advise on the feasibility and scope of requests, providing data-driven recommendations when appropriate.
Coordinate closely with the Data Architect and area lead to implement validated changes and improvements.




On-Demand Support & Incident Management

Act as the primary point of contact within the team for data‑related questions.
Troubleshoot and resolve questions about dashboards, reports, and data usage.
Manage technical incidents related to Salesforce, internal databases, and external platforms.




Data Collection, Quality & Management

Gather and consolidate data from multiple internal and external sources, including Salesforce, student surveys, LinkedIn, and other platforms.
Manage ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes to prepare data for reporting and analysis.
Maintain and update recurring reports using Excel, SharePoint lists, and Power Automate.
Support database management and continuous improvement to ensure data accuracy, quality, and completeness.




Technology & Process Improvement

Quickly learn and adopt new tools, features, and capabilities within the Microsoft ecosystem.
Test, evaluate, and integrate new analytics to enhance data processes and reporting.
Propose and implement improvements in data collection, storage, and analysis workflows to increase efficiency and quality.




Soft Skills:

Data Interpretation Advanced: Translates data into clear business recommendations and provides actionable insights.
Problem Solving Advanced: Uses critical thinking to address root causes of data discrepancies with a proactive attitude towards process improvement.
Communication Skills Advanced: Communicates business findings and solutions effectively to non-technical audiences.
Detail-oriented and capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously.




Requirements for the position:

Advanced: No specific degree is required; the most important factor we will consider is the experience working in data and analytics environments in a business facing role.
Previous experience in: minimum of 4 years working in data and analytics environments in a business facing role
Languages: English - Advanced
IT knowledge:
Excel Advanced: Creates macros, advanced referencing, and leverage Excel with other tools like Power Query.
Power Query Intermediate: Combine multiple queries using merge and append operations; optimize query steps for better performance through query folding.
Power BI Basic: Familiar with Power BI desktop, creating basic reports, and understanding key visualizations.
Understanding of CRM data structures, particularly within Salesforce.




What we offer:

Holidays & Free time: More than 35 working days of holidays.

Professional Development Opportunities: Free access to platforms with first level digital content.

Positive Working Environment: A high-collaborative and dynamic environment where the well-being is our top priority.

Conciliation:

2 days of remote working policy
Flexible working days.
You will enjoy intensive working hours all Fridays of the year, Christmas time, Easter Week and the whole months of July and August.

Other social benefits:

Flexible Remuneration: transport, childcare, training & health insurance.
Pension plan.
Attractive discounts on training programs for both employees and their inmediate family.
Great discounts at the campus restaurant.
Exclusive discounts.
Company - subsidized parking




Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

At ESADE, we are committed to diversity and provide equal professional opportunities to everyone. We foster inclusive learning and consider talent a key factor in promoting diversity. For this reason, applications from individuals with diverse abilities will be particularly valued.




We inform you that the Fundació ESADE, as the data controller, will process your data for the purpose of managing your application. Interested parties may exercise their rights to access, rectify, and delete data, as well as request that the processing of their personal data be limited, object to it, request data portability, avoid being subject to individual automated decisions when applicable, and withdraw consent at any time by contacting the Data Protection Officer at the address Av. Pedralbes, 60-62, 08034 Barcelona, and including a copy of a document that verifies their identity (DNI/NIE).




Additional and detailed information about Data Protection can be found here: https://www.esade.edu/es/proteccion-datos.

Title: BUSINESS & DATA ANALYST
Description:
Grupo HD Covalco es una empresa líder en el sector de distribución y alimentación, con una red consolidada de supermercados y franquicias en toda España. Nuestro objetivo es ofrecer productos de calidad y un servicio excepcional, adaptándonos a las necesidades de nuestros clientes y comunidades.

Estamos buscando un/a BUSINESS & DATA ANALYST para la central de Granollers.




Responsabilidades

Análisis de negocio y reporting

- Elaboración de informes sobre ventas, clientes y centros.

- Desarrollo de dashboards en Power BI.

- Reportes a Dirección.

Gestión avanzada de datos

- Uso avanzado de Excel (tablas dinámicas, Power Query, fórmulas).

- Uso de IA para automatización y análisis.

- Mejora de estructuras de datos.

Optimización de procesos

- Automatización de tareas repetitivas.

- Reducción de tiempos de análisis.

- Estandarización de informes.

Ejecución operativa de compras

- Gestión de repartos definidos.

- Seguimiento de incidencias.

- Coordinación con Compras.

Soporte comercial y Call Center

- Atención a centros y clientes.

- Resolución de dudas comerciales.

- Gestión y seguimiento de incidencias.




Requisitos

-Formación: Grado en ADE o Economía

-Experiencia previa en distribución

-Conocimiento del mercado

-Dominio Excel

-Residir por la zona




Habilidades

-Capacidad analítica

-Organización y planificación

-Comunicación

-Proactividad

-Adaptabilidad




Qué ofrecemos?

-Contrato indefinido

-Jornada completa

-Paquete retributivo competitivo

-Incorporación inmediata

-Formar parte de una gran compañía en expansión




Si estás interesado/a en la oferta laboral contacta con nosotros. ¡Queremos conocerte!




En Grupo HD Covalco, promovemos un entorno de trabajo inclusivo y respetuoso, en el que cada persona puede desarrollar su potencial al máximo. Title: Fraud Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Satispay began by rethinking the simple act of a payment to remove the friction from our daily routines. But we didn't stop there. Today, we are building a complete financial platform designed to empower people and concretely improve their lives.

By giving our 6 million users a clear, open path to pay, save, and invest, we are evolving into the definitive destination for every financial need.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Fraud Data Analyst, you'll be the person who secures the future of digital payments by architecting sophisticated real-time defense strategies.

Here's What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like


Architect Real-Time Strategies – You will be responsible for our real-time decisioning engine, designing and deploying logic that combines ML scores with velocity features to make millisecond decisions.
Precision Rule Development – Translate complex fraud patterns into high-performing rules, optimizing the balance between False Positive Rates (FPR) and Gross Fraud Loss.
Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.


Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?


Experience – You have between 3 and 7 years of professional experience, specifically within the risk, payments, or credit sectors.
Technical Proficiency – You are highly skilled in SQL and Python for data analysis and strategy development.
Domain Expertise – You have a solid understanding of payment fraud dynamics and experience working with real-time decisioning environments.
Communication – You possess excellent communication skills and are fluent in English.
Nice to Have – Experience with Machine Learning, Data Science, PySpark, or GraphQL knowledge is a significant plus.


CareAbout: how we support your impact

About

We move fast, and evolution never stops. It's a fun ride, but it can be challenging. To make sure our people truly thrive, we're committed to making their lives easier, both in the office and out in the world. That's why we created CareAbout:


❤️ Health (Private insurance for you and your family, psychological support with Serenis, mental health workshops)
💰 Financial resources (Stock Option Plan, Meal vouchers, Relocation support if you're moving countries)
⚙️ Growth and development (Professional development programs, Internal mobility, Language courses with Preply)
🌱 Flexibility (Unlimited PTO, Hybrid working policy*, Flexible working hours)
👨‍👩‍👧 Family (Enhanced parental leave, Additional leave for child sickness)
We embrace three days per week in-office (Tuesday and Thursday + 1 of your choice), with the option to request extra remote time.


Equal Opportunity Employer

At Satispay, we're proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and inclusion, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds. This opportunity is open to everyone, regardless - for instance - of race, colour, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, and national origin. Join us in a workplace where everyone belongs!

Learn more about us

Our values and pillars aren't just fancy words on a page - they really shape everything we do. Explore them here.

By submitting this application, I acknowledge that I have read and understood the content of the Privacy Policy

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Role

As a Data Analyst, you'll help our customers get up and running on Affinity by importing their data and setting up the platform to support their workflows. You'll work closely with our Onboarding, Implementation, and Customer Success teams, and play a key role in communicating directly with customers to gather requirements, set expectations, and deliver clear, actionable reports that demonstrate the value of their data setup.

What you'll do:


Import and transform customer CSV data into Affinity and configure tables, lists, and fields so that customers can hit the ground running.
Manage the data import queue with discipline — tracking status, ETA, priority, and next steps for every import.
Clean, deduplicate, and validate data to ensure high-quality imports.
Work with Onboarding and Implementation teams to map customer data to Affinity's data model.
Lead requirements-gathering conversations with customers to understand their data structures, workflows, and reporting needs.
Translate customer requirements into well-structured data configurations and reports that align with their business goals.
Build and deliver clear reports and dashboards for customers that surface key insights from their imported data.
Communicate proactively with customers to clarify data formats, align on timelines, and keep stakeholders informed throughout the onboarding process.
Leverage AI tools to automate and templatize repeatable workflows.


What we're looking for:

Don't meet every single requirement? At Affinity, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, we encourage you to apply anyways.


Excel / Google Sheets power user — comfortable with VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, pivot tables, and text manipulation.
Strong client communication skills, with the ability to lead requirements-gathering conversations, ask the right questions, and translate business needs into technical solutions.
Confident communicator who can present data findings and reports to non-technical stakeholders in a clear, concise way.
Strong attention to detail with a quality-first mindset.
Familiarity with CRM platforms or database concepts (fields, records, relationships).
Good communication skills.
Organized and able to manage multiple imports with competing deadlines.
Manage the data import queue with discipline — tracking status, ETA, priority, and next steps for every import.
Experience with data analytics, reporting, or dashboard building (Looker, Tableau, or similar).


Nice to have:


Experience in B2B SaaS, customer onboarding, or professional services.


About Affinity

With more than 3,000 customers worldwide and backed by some of Silicon Valley's best firms, Affinity has raised $120M to empower dealmakers to find, manage, and close more deals. How? Our Relationship Intelligence platform uses the wealth of data exhaust from trillions of interactions between Investment Bankers, Venture Capitalists, Consultants, and other strategic dealmakers to deliver automated relationship insights that drive over 450,000 deals every month. We are are proud to have received Inc. and Fortune Best Workplaces awards as well as to be Great Places to Work certified for the last 5 years running. Join us on our mission to make it possible for anyone to cultivate and fully harness their network to succeed.

We use E-Verify

Our company uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit www.dhs.gov/E-Verify.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Company Description 

Ona Therapeutics is a biotech company located in Barcelona (Parc Científic de Barcelona, PCB), focused on developing first-in-class antibody drug conjugates (ADCs) for advanced cancer treatment. By partnering with leading academics and clinicians, we identify novel therapeutic targets from the unique biology of treatment refractory advanced cancer patients, to provide effective new treatments for patients with no remaining alternatives. 

 

Scope 

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst with a strong scientific background to support data-driven decision-making across our project portfolio and strategic initiatives. 




This role sits at the intersection of science, data, and project strategy, supporting the Head of Project & Business Management in transforming complex biological, clinical, and industry data into structured insights that guide internal decision-making. 




You will play a key role in strengthening how the company uses data to evaluate priorities, track programs, and understand the competitive landscape. 

 

Role and Responsibilities  

Business Intelligence & Data Analysis  

Develop a deep understanding of the biotech/pharma landscape, including competitors, modalities, and therapeutic trends 
Gather, structure, and analyse scientific, clinical, and regulatory data (clinical trials, publications, databases, etc.) 
Translate complex datasets into clear insights, summaries, and decision-support materials 
Support portfolio and project teams by providing data-driven insights for prioritisation and planning 
Contribute to the definition of key analytical frameworks and indicators for project and portfolio evaluation 
Support the Head of Project & Business Management in preparing data-driven analyses for internal decision-making 




Strategic Insight & External Analysis 

Support evaluation of external opportunities (assets, indications, collaborations) through structured data analysis 
Contribute to competitive intelligence and landscape mapping 
Assist in preparing analytical inputs for strategic discussions and presentations 




Scientific Integration 

Participate in internal scientific and project meetings 
Collaborate closely with R&D and project management teams to understand data needs 
Contribute to a data-driven culture across project execution and decision-making 




Tools & Data Systems 

Support the structuring and maintenance of internal BI and project intelligence databases 
Work with scientific databases, literature sources, and internal data systems 
Contribute to improving how data is collected, structured, and visualised across projects 

 

Qualifications Requirements  

PhD in relevant scientific fields (Biology, Biochemistry, Pharmacology, Biomedical Sciences, etc.) 
Relevant professional experience in business intelligence highly analytical roles within biotech, consulting, venture capital, or technology transfer, demonstrating strong ability to structure complex problems, interpret data-driven insights, and support evidence-based decision-making. 
Strong interest in biotech, data analysis, and project/portfolio decision-making 
Experience in oncology, immunology, drug-development or related 

 

Skills 

Strong analytical, structured, and systematic mindset  
Excellent critical thinking and problem-solving abilities  
Data-driven with a hypothesis-oriented approach  
Ability to translate complex scientific and clinical data into clear, actionable insights  
Ability to simplify complex information and extract key messages  
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills  
Excellent communication and presentation skills  
Intellectual curiosity and proactive mindset  
Ownership mindset with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment

﻿ 

What we offer 

A work environment, culture and passionate team in the oncology sector. 
A unique opportunity to work in an innovative and dynamic environment. 
Internal benefits such as flexible remuneration, flexible working hours, events along the year... 

 

To apply, please submit your CV and cover letter to hr@ona-therapeutics.com 

We look forward to reviewing your application and discovering how your skills and experience align with our company’s needs.

Title: Lead Data Analyst - Marketing
Description:
Who we are:
At Rover, pets and their people are at the heart of everything we do. We connect pet parents with trusted pet care across the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Australia.

Headquartered in Seattle and Barcelona, we’re a values driven, fast-growing tech company focused on building safe and personalized experiences tailored to the needs of each unique pet. We’re investing in AI as a business accelerator and provide every team member access to AI tools in service of creating better experiences for our community.

We are proud to be recognized as a great place to work, having been named among the 100 Best Companies to Work For in Seattle Business Magazine and Washington’s Best Workplaces in the Puget Sound Business Journal. At Rover we’re committed to creating an accessible, inclusive, and welcoming community, which starts with our employees.

Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.

Who We're Looking For:
We're looking for a Lead Analyst to ensure that our Marketing team makes data-driven decisions at every stage—from early-stage ROI modeling to designing and analyzing experiments that drive global growth. You will be the primary point of contact for our Growth, SEO, and International teams, collaborating directly with marketing leadership to define strategy.

You will take the lead in designing measurement frameworks and analyzing performance to inform where we invest next. You will be empowered to identify the root causes of fluctuations in our metrics and present the overarching narrative of marketplace performance to the broader organization. When the data shows a need for change, you are expected to bring together the evidence and the right people to find the solution.

This is a hybrid position that has the minimum expectation to work out of our Barcelona Office in the Poblenou neighborhood two days per week on Mondays and Thursdays. For this reason, candidates must be based in Barcelona or willing to relocate.

🎉 Your Impact:


Deepen customer engagement: Lead the analysis on customer retention and LTV expansion. You will identify underlying trends and proactively recommend strategies to the marketing team to increase long-term user value
Tell stories that drive action: Design the reporting architecture and define the key business metrics for the department. You will build dashboards and craft data narratives that directly influence decisions made by marketing and cross-functional leadership
Drive marketing efficiency: Define the way we run experimentation in the Marketing space. You will advance our testing methodology to ensure statistical rigor and optimize strategies across multiple marketing functions
Partner with key decision-makers: Partner with Growth, International Expansion, and SEO leadership. You will translate their complex business goals into analytical roadmaps and provide the insights needed to prioritize their initiatives




🙌 Your Qualifications:


6+ years of experience using data to drive business impact, including identifying actionable insights, crafting compelling narratives, and partnering with cross-functional teams to define and implement solutions
Advanced proficiency in SQL, with the ability to review and optimize code from other analysts and collaborate with engineering on data models
Strong proficiency in statistical analysis and using Python or R
Proven expertise in A/B testing, statistical analysis, and experimental design
Proven ability to manage large analytical initiatives from design to completion, including managing stakeholder expectations and timelines
Demonstrated success in collaborating cross-functionally to define business problems, scope project deliverables and timelines, implement reporting solutions, and shape organizational strategy
Highly adept communicator of data insights across technical and non-technical audiences
Data visualisation tool experience (e.g. Mode, Looker, Tableau, etc.)




💪 Nice to Have:


Demonstrated experience mentoring and developing junior team members, analysts, and non-technical stakeholders
Experience in a two-sided marketplace




🐶 Benefits of working at Rover:


Long-term incentive plan with a company performance-based cash payout
Pension plan
Private medical insurance
25 days PTO
Meal allowance and flexible compensation plan (transport and nursery)
Gym membership
€450 to cover the costs associated with the adoption of a pet
Annual €150 wellness reimbursement
Flexible work hours, sometimes you'll need to be in at certain times, but on the whole, we're pretty flexible when it comes to managing workload and time
Grab snacks, fresh fruit, in our kitchen to keep yourself going
Regular team activities, events, game nights, and more
Dog-friendly office




Rover is an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting a diverse, inclusive, and inventive environment with the best employees. We’re driven by seeing our people succeed and grow, and we work to ensure everyone contributes to their fullest potential. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, protected veteran status, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances.

_

Title: Business Intelligence Data Analyst (Power BI)
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
En BETWEEN apostamos por ofrecerte las mejores oportunidades dentro del área tecnológica. Contamos con un equipo de más de 1000 profesionales.

Si quieres desarrollar tus capacidades en un entorno internacional, apostando por trabajar en soluciones de I+D tanto en Ingeniería como en IT, ¡este es tu próximo destino!

Damos soporte en una gran variedad de áreas como Operaciones, Desarrollo de producto, Compras, Firmware, Software, Business Intelligence, Gestión, Transformación digital, ¡y muchas más! Además, desarrollamos soluciones tecnológicas a medida desde nuestros Hubs Tecnológicos.

La persona seleccionada formará parte de un equipo multidisciplinar, donde se valora especialmente la calidad, la innovación y la excelencia técnica, en un entorno de I+D orientado a la mejora continua y al desarrollo de soluciones de alto impacto para el negocio.

¿Qué haré? Estas serán tus funciones principales:


Diseñar, desarrollar y mantener dashboards avanzados en Power BI, asegurando altos estándares de calidad, rendimiento y usabilidad.
Analizar y resolver incidencias en los dashboards existentes, garantizando su estabilidad y fiabilidad.
Realizar el modelado de datos, optimizando estructuras y flujos de información.
Desarrollar y optimizar consultas SQL para la extracción, transformación y análisis de datos.
Colaborar en un entorno internacional, utilizando inglés oral de forma habitual en el día a día.


¿Cómo lo haré?


Tendrás contrato indefinido y horario flexible
Podrás trabajar en modelo híbrido. Trabajarás 3 días a la semana en la oficina de Sant Cugat del Vallès. El resto de días puedes teletrabajar.
Tendrás un PC y los periféricos necesarios para el desarrollo de tu trabajo.
Trabajarás en un entorno internacional.


Si Eres BETWEENER Obtendrás Estos Beneficios


Seguro médico con Alan Seguros.
Dispondrás de oportunidades de formación y desarrollo profesional: Idiomas, cursos en Udemy, Certificaciones, Programa The Power Business School y Bootcamps especializados (Agile, DevOps, IA & Big Data, Ciberseguridad, etc.).
Contarás con un equipo de Talento que te acompañará durante toda tu experiencia con nosotros. ¡Haremos un café una vez al mes! Así, mantendremos el contacto.
Pondremos a tu disposición un amplio abanico de retos y proyectos acordes con tus objetivos personales y profesionales.
Posibilidad de elegir cómo obtener parte de tu salario gracias a las ventajas fiscales de nuestra Retribución Flexible (Ticket restaurant, Ticket transporte, Ticket guardería).
Descuentos para acceder a centros de Fitness con Wellhub.
Acceso a nuestro Club de Descuentos: Inspiring Benefits.
En BETWEEN nos gusta mucho hacer networking, por lo que te invitaremos a participar en todos estos eventos ¡y más!: las BETWEEN Nights, torneos deportivos, etc.
Programa remunerado de recomendación de Talento.


Requisitos

Requisitos imprescindibles


Grado en ADE, Ingeniería o titulaciones afines.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años trabajando con Power BI (modelado de datos, DAX, Power Query, etc.).
Sólidos conocimientos en SQL.
Inglés avanzado (mínimo C1), con capacidad para comunicarse fluidamente en entornos profesionales.


Requisitos valorables


Experiencia en entornos de I+D o equipos multidisciplinares.
Experiencia en procesos de ETL / ELT y orquestación de datos (por ejemplo, Azure Data Factory, Airflow u otros).
Conocimientos en lenguajes backend (Python) o plataformas de datos (Databricks).
Capacidad analítica y enfoque a negocio.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Perk (formerly TravelPerk) is the intelligent platform for travel and spend management. Built to tackle the time-consuming, manual work that gets in the way of real work, our tools automate everything from travel bookings to expenses, invoice processing, and more. By eliminating this shadow work that wastes hours, erodes morale, and saps innovation, we’re on a mission to power real work, with real impact.

We’re trusted by more than 10,000 companies worldwide, including Wise, On Running, Breitling, and Fabletics, and we’re tackling the 7 hours of lost productivity per employee each week, a $1.7 trillion problem.

Founded in 2015, Perk has grown into a global company of more than 1,800 people across 12 offices globally, with headquarters in London and Boston. We combine innovation, control, and simplicity to transform how businesses work and how people feel at work.

At Perk, we’re driven by our values, like being an owner, delivering a 7-star experience, and working as one team. We value curiosity, purpose, and mindset, not just knowledge, to unlock the power in your potential. Our talent team brings together leading minds from the travel and SaaS industries, representing over 70 countries. If you’re excited about having a real impact and shaping how millions of people experience work, we’d love you on the team.

Visit www.perk.com to learn more.

Data Analyst - Job Description

We are looking for a talented Data Analyst to join our team. You will be leveraging data to generate impactful insights for strategic purposes. This is a pivotal position to bring the business to the next level, you will support one of our business domain teams using analytics to drive better decisions at Perk.

What you will do:


Work with other Data Analysts, Analytics Engineers, Data Scientists and Engineers in our team to generate advanced analysis that answers key business questions and unlocks opportunities.
Explore data from different sources, turning it into information, information into insight and insight into decisions
Design and develop metrics, dashboards, experiments and analyses to track and assess the impact of new initiatives and features.
Develop data frameworks and products to drive insights and behavioural analysis.


What you need:


2+ years of relevant experience interpreting data in data analytics or data science role, including relevant tools, techniques and technology
Excellent communication skills, both written and spoken, in English


You will need to prove the following:


You are a Master using SQL for data querying, transformation and modelling - experience with dbt is a strong plus.
Experience with statistical concepts and packages (Python / R)
Experience with data visualisation and BI tools (Looker)
Data storytelling and stakeholder management experience
The desire to work in an international environment, with minimal direction, alongside highly engaged individuals.


How We Work

At Perk, we take an IRL-first approach to work, where our team works together in-person 3 days a week. As such, this role requires you to be based within commuting distance of our hubs. We fundamentally believe in the value of meeting in real life to improve connectivity, productivity, creativity and ultimately making us a great place to work.

For certain roles, we can help with relocation from anywhere in the world, English is the official language at the office. Please submit your resume in English if you choose to apply. Do not forget to submit an updated portfolio and/or resume.

Perk is a global company with a diverse customer base, and we want to make sure the people behind our product reflect that. We’re an equal opportunity employer, which means you’re welcome at Perk regardless of how you look, where you’re from, or anything else that makes you, well, you.

Protect Yourself from Recruitment Scams

All official communication from Perk will always come from email addresses ending in @perk.com or @externalperk.com, our verified social media channels, or recruiters listed on our official LinkedIn page. We will never ask you to pay for equipment, training, or fees, request sensitive personal information such as bank details early in the process, or communicate through unofficial apps like WhatsApp, Telegram, or Signal. If you receive a message claiming to be from Perk that seems suspicious, please do not respond. Forward it to security [at] perk .com, and we will confirm whether it is legitimate.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
About Us

With over 185 years of history, we are a leading health and wellness company. Our portfolio includes iconic brands such as Aquilea, Fisiocrem, Laborest, Sidroga, Biodramina, Aerored, Fave de Fuca, Halibut, Ems, and Depuralina, with a strong presence across major European markets.

We believe in the power of nature, enhanced by science, as the best way to improve human health and wellbeing sustainably. Our goal is to create a positive impact on both people's lives and the planet. We are currently seeking a highly motivated and skilled Data Analyst to join our team.

Your Mission

We are currently looking to strengthen our Data team by appointing a Data Analyst who will play a key role in designing and delivering scalable, high-quality reporting solutions across the organization. As part of the Data Department, you will work cross-functionally with business stakeholders and Data Engineering, acting as a trusted partner between data and decision-making. You will lead end-to-end analytics projects, drive best practices, and contribute to the continuous evolution of our data platform while mentoring more junior team members.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

In this role, you will be expected to:


Lead end-to-end reporting projects autonomously, from requirements gathering and data modeling to development, validation, and deployment in Power BI
Design and maintain optimized semantic data models (e.g., star schema, reusable DAX measures) to support multiple business use cases
Act as an analytical business partner, proactively identifying insights, KPIs, and opportunities beyond initial stakeholder requests
Ensure data quality, consistency, and performance of dashboards, troubleshooting and optimizing complex models when needed
Collaborate closely with Data Engineering teams to define and improve data models and architectures aligned with reporting needs
Drive reporting automation, documentation standards, and support/mentor business users and junior analysts in dashboard usage and best practices


Sector: Industria farmacéutica

Función: Ingeniería / Proyectos

Tipo de empleo: Trabajo a tiempo indefinido

Nivel profesional: Administrativo / Oficina

You'll excel if you have...


3-5 years of experience in Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or similar roles, with at least 2 years of hands-on experience working intensively with Power BI in production environments
Advanced expertise in Power BI, including semantic modeling, intermediate-advanced DAX (time intelligence, filter context, CALCULATE, variables), Power Query (M), and performance optimization
Strong SQL skills (complex queries with multiple joins, CTEs, window functions, subqueries, and advanced aggregations), combined with solid knowledge of dimensional data modeling (star schema, SCDs, granularity)
Experience designing reports for different audiences (operational to executive), applying best practices in data visualization and storytelling
Strong ownership and autonomy, with the ability to lead projects end-to-end, manage multiple priorities, and ensure high-quality outputs with rigorous attention to detail
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to challenge requirements and translate business needs into scalable analytical solutions
A proactive, curious, and improvement-driven mindset, with experience mentoring junior profiles and collaborating across technical and non-technical teams
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks), Medallion architectures (Bronze/Silver/Gold), version control (Git), or DataOps practices is a plus
Experience with other BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Qlik), systems such as SAP/Salesforce/Odoo, or programming in Python for data analysis is considered an advantage
Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Administration, Economics, or equivalent; relevant certifications (e.g., PL-300, DP-600) are a plus
Fluent English (minimum B2 spoken) and Spanish, additional languages are a plus
Show more

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We are looking for an exceptional Data Analyst to join the Coches.Net, Motos.Net and Milanuncios.com team. You will work as a member of multidisciplinary team, collaborating closely with Product owners, cluster teams, data engineers and management team stake-holders (Marketing, Sales, Product, BizDev and other Operations teams).

Your main mission is to define and execute analyses of several business context to identify trends, opportunities and problems to improve the offering and services we offer.

Job Requirements


3+ years of experience in eCommerce, an online company.
Advanced knowledge of SQL and relational databases
Advanced knowledge of Python and/or R for data analysis (e.g. pandas, numpy, matplotlib), and advanced analytics (e.g. scikit-learn)
Advanced user of at least one visualisation tool such as Tableau and/or QlikSense.
Analytical skills: know how to interpret data, to extract relevant insights and be able to translate business questions into analytical needs Nice-to-have
Experience working with sales, operations, and strategy teams.
Good knowledge of basic machine learning algorithms (e.g. clustering, classification, regressions)


Nice To Have


Experience working with sales, operations, and strategy teams.
Good knowledge of basic machine learning algorithms (e.g. clustering, classification, regressions)
Some experience with big data technology (e.g. databricks, datalakes)
Some knowledge of data engineering tools (e.g. Airflow, DBT)


Job Responsibilities


Identify, analyse and interpret KPIs and Health Metrics related to our users and customers, creating dashboard and visualisation to monitor them.
Identifying trends, risks and opportunities to provide insights that can make recommendations on how to improve the different initiatives of the vertical.
Create powerful Insights using Advanced analytics to improve the knowledge of our users and customers.
Actively participate in the discovery, and development of advanced analytical models (such as clustering, and predictive models), into their implementation in business use cases, such as customer segmentation, and propensity models for upselling, downselling, and churn prediction.

Title: Analista de sostenibilidad
Description:
En Oxford Global Resources estamos buscando un perfil de Analista de sostenibilidad, para un cliente líder en el sector farmacéutico. Se trata de un proyecto por ahora confirmado hasta final de año 2026 con posibilidad de continuidad.




¿Te apasiona la sostenibilidad y te motiva trabajar con datos?

Formarás parte del equipo de sostenibilidad apoyando en tareas clave relacionadas con:




Recopilación y consolidación de datos de huella de carbono desde distintas áreas de la compañía.
Validación de datos: detectar errores, inconsistencias y entender por qué suben o bajan ciertos valores.
Revisión de documentación de ecodiseño y PPWR vinculada a envases y cumplimiento normativo.
Seguimiento del registro de proyectos para asegurar que las áreas cargan la información correctamente.
Validación de ahorros de CO₂ asociados a los proyectos.
Coordinación con propietarios de datos para asegurar trazabilidad y calidad de la información.







🧩 ¿Qué perfil encaja?

Buscamos un perfil con ganas de aprender y que disfrute trabajando con datos.
Gusto por la gestión de datos, Excel y trabajo estructurado.
Capacidad para hablar con distintas áreas y hacer seguimiento proactivo.
Atención al detalle y curiosidad por entender el “por qué” detrás de los números.
Inglés B2–C1.




📍 Condiciones

50% teletrabajo
Incorporación inmediata.







Si te motiva crecer en sostenibilidad desde un rol muy práctico, orientado a datos y con impacto real, este proyecto puede ser tu punto de partida. Estamos buscando a alguien con ganas, curiosidad y actitud.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción de la empresa

MSX International es un líder global en servicios para el sector de automoción, presente en más de 80 países. Desde nuestra sede en Barcelona trabajamos con diferentes fabricantes internacionales para ofrecer soluciones innovadoras que impulsan la eficiencia, la calidad y el rendimiento de sus operaciones.

Si buscas un entorno dinámico, analítico, internacional y orientado a la mejora continua, este es tu sitio.

Descripción del empleo

Como Analista de Datos, serás responsable de gestionar, analizar y transformar datos clave relacionados con proyectos del sector automoción. Tu misión será convertir grandes volúmenes de datos en insights claros y accionables que apoyen la toma de decisiones, la optimización de procesos y el rendimiento general del negocio.

Requisitos


Realizar análisis diarios, semanales y mensuales utilizando herramientas como Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Identificar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora
Preparar informes avanzados alineados con KPIs y requisitos del proyecto
Mantener, actualizar y asegurar la calidad de la documentación y reportes en herramientas como SharePoint y Office365
Identificar patrones en los datos y proponer acciones correctivas u oportunidades de optimización
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para convertir insights en acciones concretas
Garantizar el cumplimiento de estándares internos en informes y dashboards
Presentar análisis, conclusiones e información relevante a equipos internos y dirección
Asegurar la precisión, consistencia y calidad de los datos analizados
Proponer mejoras continuas en procesos, metodologías y flujos de análisis


Requisitos

Conocimientos y habilidades


Habilidades de comunicación y capacidad para trabajar con equipos diversos
Enfoque resolutivo para problemas complejos
Capacidad para gestionar prioridades y trabajar con plazos ajustados
Dominio de Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Conocimientos de sistemas de gestión de datos, minería de datos, procesos ETL y reporting automatizado
Competencias en análisis estadístico y modelos de rendimiento
Experiencia con herramientas de automatización (RPA) es un plus
Conocimiento de Microsoft Dynamics (deseable)


Formación


Grado en Ingeniería, Economía, Estadística, Matemáticas o formación equivalente


Experiencia


Experiencia previa demostrable como Analista de Datos


Idiomas


Español nativo
Inglés B2 (escrito y hablado)


Información adicional

Qué ofrecemos


Puesto permanente con contrato indefinido desde el primer día.
Política de teletrabajo de hasta el 50% de la jornada mensual tras el periodo de adaptación.
Formar parte de un gran equipo de profesionales en una compañía internacional consolidada.
Obtener una visión global del sector automoción, interactuando con clientes, proveedores, consultores y concesionarios.
Salario: 23.000 euros brutos anuales.
Acceso a MSX Bazaar, el portal de compras colectivas con grandes descuentos para empleados.
Posibilidad de adherirte al Plan de Retribución Flexible (seguro médico, tarjeta transporte, tarjeta restaurante, formación...).
23 días laborables de vacaciones al año según convenio y 3 días por año completo trabajado.
Incentivos adicionales por programas de recomendación interna, formadores internos, detección de nuevas oportunidades de negocio, etc.


¿Crees que eres la persona ideal para este puesto? ¡Queremos conocerte! Envíanos tu candidatura ahora.

MSX es un empleador que promueve la igualdad de oportunidades y alienta positivamente las solicitudes de candidatos debidamente cualificados y elegibles, independientemente de su sexo, raza, discapacidad, edad, orientación sexual, cambio de sexo, religión o creencias, estado civil, embarazo o maternidad. Como usuarios del programa de confianza en la discapacidad, garantizamos entrevistar a todos los candidatos discapacitados que cumplan los criterios mínimos para la vacante.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

We are looking for a switched‑on Data Analyst to manage and evolve our reporting pipelines, analytics and automation workflows across European digital transformation unit supporting sales and marketing programs.

This role goes beyond traditional reporting. It combines designing, building, and maintaining end-to-end data pipelines ensuring high‑quality, standardized and scalable reporting for the business.

This role has a strong focus on reporting ownership, automation and data quality, while working closely with business stakeholders and technology teams to ensure data is translated into reliable insight and effective decision‑making.

Apply if


You think you are good match for most of the requirements in the job description.
You are a team player, and you enjoy working in a multicultural team.
You approach business and technical issues with flexibility and rigor.
You are comfortable owning reporting end‑to‑end, including recurring deliverables, data validation, fixing issues, and improving existing dashboards - not just designing new ones.
You like having clear ownership and accountability rather than jumping between loosely defined initiatives.
You are comfortable working close to the business, translating requests into concrete analytical solutions, even when requirements are incomplete at first.
You can work independently, manage priorities, and take responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks.
You see automation, standardization and scalability as core parts of your professional identity, not as side work.
You are curious about experimenting with emerging AI capabilities in analytics environments.


Key Responsibilities

Reporting and Analytics foundations


End‑to‑end ownership of reporting frameworks and data pipelines
Enabling consistent, decision‑ready reporting across the European business through automated reporting and ad-hoc analysis.
Ensure a high level of data quality, consistency and reliability across all reports.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of reporting infrastructure and metrics definitions.


Automation and process improvement


Identify opportunities to streamline, automate and optimize analytics and data processes.
Design, execute and continuously improve scalable and automated reporting and analytics solutions.
Define and document data pipelines, dependencies and sources.
Coordinate with IT and Data Platform teams on the implementation of automation and enhancements.
Support sustainable and well‑governed processes rather than ad‑hoc solutions.


Business Partnership & Enablement


Work closely with business stakeholders translate business objectives into clear analytical requirements and reporting specifications.
Support digital transformation initiatives by providing trusted data and analytics foundations.


Reporting & Analysis


Support insight generation related to customer behavior and business performance, in collaboration with advanced analytics or specialist teams where relevant.
Own the end-to-end lifecycle of dashboards and reporting
Perform structured and ad‑hoc analysis to support business questions and priorities.


AI-Enhanced Analytics


Explore opportunities to leverage Generative AI to, automate reporting narratives and insights generation, improve data, accelerate development of data pipelines.


Required Experience

Experience, skills & profile


Proven experience in a Business Data Analyst, BI Analyst or Analytics role.
Proven ability to collaborate with Data Engineers and Data Analysts to build infrastructure.
ELT mindset and familiar with dbt concepts.
Comfortable with Python for data processing, scripting and automation.
Experience working with Cloud Data Warehouse (ideally Snowflake)
Advanced SQL skills (Data modeling basics, complex joins and transformations, query optimization)
Hands‑on experience creating and maintaining reports and dashboards using Power BI and DAX.


Personal attributes


Comfortable owning analytics deliverables end‑to‑end.
Able to drive clarity in ambiguous situations.
Comfortable working in a multicultural, cross‑functional environment as a team player
Professional written and spoken English is required.
EU citizenship or valid work permit required.


Working Conditions


Hybrid work model: 2 days in the office and 3 days remote per week.


Key Skills

Accountability, Collaboration, Data Analysis, Data Pipelines, Data Processing, Process Improvements, Report Analysis, Report Automation

At TD SYNNEX, our values guide everything we do: Together, We Own It, We Dare to Go, We Grow and Win, and above all, We Do the Right Thing. These principles shape how we work with each other, our partners, and our communities as we drive innovation and create lasting impact.

What's In It For You?


Elective Benefits: Our programs are tailored to your country to best accommodate your lifestyle.
Grow Your Career: Accelerate your path to success (and keep up with the future) with formal programs on leadership and professional development, and many more on-demand courses.
Elevate Your Personal Well-Being: Boost your financial, physical, and mental well-being through seminars, events, and our global Life Empowerment Assistance Program.
Diversity, Equity & Inclusion: It's not just a phrase to us; valuing every voice is how we succeed. Join us in celebrating our global diversity through inclusive education, meaningful peer-to-peer conversations, and equitable growth and development opportunities.
Make the Most of our Global Organization: Network with other new co-workers within your first 30 days through our onboarding program.
Connect with Your Community: Participate in internal, peer-led inclusive communities and activities, including business resource groups, local volunteering events, and more environmental and social initiatives.


Don't meet every single requirement? Apply anyway.

At TD SYNNEX, we're proud to be recognized as a great place to work and a leader in the promotion and practice of diversity, equity and inclusion. If you're excited about working for our company and believe you're a good fit for this role, we encourage you to apply. You may be exactly the person we're looking for!
