Title: Data Analyst (Engineer)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Scientists and Analysts work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can dig into our complex databases, look for the root cause of a problem, and design their own solutions by writing code to implement them.

This is an opportunity to apply your skills while picking up new ones and delivering value for millions of customers around the world.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and services teams
Developing documentation and data governance
Owning the entire ETL process
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Providing clean data sets, modelling data in a way that empowers end users to answer their own questions


What You'll Need


5+ years of experience in an analytical role, creating impactful data-driven solutions
A solid background/education in a quantitative discipline
Great skills with Python, SQL, or other programming languages
Evidence of exceptional mathematical and statistics knowledge


Nice to have


An advanced degree in a core STEM subject
Experience with additional programming languages (Java, Scala, C++, etc.)
School/university Olympic medal competitions in physics, maths, economics, or programming


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Únete a Amaris Consulting y participa en iniciativas data-driven que impactan a millones de usuarias de moda en todo el mundo. Formarás parte de un grupo internacional, trabajando implant (FTE) en el equipo de Ecommerce en un entorno que valora la innovación, la autonomía y el talento, utilizando un stack de datos real y de última generación.

🎯 Tu Misión & Impacto

Como Data analyst te incorporarás al equipo de Ecommerce para transformar datos en insights accionables, modelos analíticos y recomendaciones estratégicas que impulsen la captación, conversión, venta y retención de clientas, así como la reducción de devoluciones.

Trabajarás mano a mano con stakeholders de negocio digital, CRM, paid media y tecnología, asegurando que las decisiones clave se basen en datos y contribuyendo directamente al crecimiento del canal online.

🛠 Responsabilidades



Analizar datos de clientes y comportamiento digital para mejorar:Captación, conversión, venta, fidelización y devoluciones.
Diseñar, crear y mantener dashboards y reporting en Power BI para los equipos de Ecommerce y negocio.
Definir y hacer seguimiento de KPIs clave de Ecommerce (tráfico, conversión, AOV, churn, LTV, etc.) para apoyar la toma de decisiones estratégicas.
Integrar y cruzar datos digitales (web/app) con:CRM, paid media y datos transaccionales.
Desarrollar modelos analíticos y predictivos orientados a negocio:Segmentación de clientes, scoring, propensión a compra, forecasting de demanda, etc.
Velar por la calidad del dato, proponiendo mejoras en procesos y fuentes de información.
Preparar y comunicar insights y resultados de manera clara a los distintos stakeholders (Ecommerce, Marketing, Producto, Tech).
Colaborar en iniciativas de experimentación digital (A/B testing) para optimizar funnels, campañas y experiencias de usuario.



✅ Requisitos



Experiencia previa en entornos Ecommerce (retail, moda, marketplaces o similares).
Dominio avanzado de SQL.
Experiencia trabajando con BigQuery y Snowflake.
Dominio avanzado de Power BI (modelado de datos, DAX, visualizaciones).
Nivel avanzado de Excel.
Nivel alto de inglés (entorno internacional, documentación y reuniones).



🌟 Se valorará positivamente



Experiencia en modelos predictivos:Propensión a compra, churn, LTV, forecasting de demanda, recomendación de productos, etc.
Conocimiento de técnicas de machine learning (supervisado y no supervisado).
Experiencia con Python (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn).
Uso de notebooks (Databricks Notebooks / Jupyter).
Experiencia en A/B testing y experimentación digital.
Uso de herramientas como GrowthBook y Optimizely.
Experiencia trabajando con Azure DevOps y flujos de trabajo colaborativos (versionado, CI/CD de modelos o pipelines).



🧩 Tech Stack

Trabajarás con un stack moderno y orientado a grandes volúmenes de datos:



Data & Cloud: BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks
Lenguajes: SQL, Python
Reporting & BI: Power BI, Excel
Experimentación: GrowthBook, Optimizely
Colaboración & DevOps: Azure DevOps



🏙 Ubicación & Modelo de trabajo



Posición basada en Cerdanyola del Valles, Barcelona, España.
Se dispone de autobus de empresa.
Presencialidad 2 días a la semana.
Entorno multidisciplinar con fuerte foco en negocio digital, datos y tecnología.



🚀 Por qué unirte a Amaris Consulting



Crecimiento profesional: el 70% de nuestros líderes senior comenzaron en posiciones junior.
Formación continua: acceso a academias internas, formaciones online, webinars y certificaciones patrocinadas por la compañía.
Flexibilidad & beneficios: modelo de trabajo híbrido y paquete competitivo de beneficios sociales (a detallar durante el proceso).
Bienestar: nuestro programa WeCare te acompaña en situaciones críticas.
Desarrollo personal: presupuesto anual de formación para potenciar tus habilidades y acelerar tu carrera.



📅 Fecha de incorporación: ASAP

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.

Take the next step in your career and become a key player in driving change at Amaris Consulting. Apply now to embark on a journey of professional growth and personal fulfillment.

Who are we?

Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1,000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:

Brief Call: Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. Job Description

Únete a Amaris Consulting y participa en iniciativas data-driven que impactan a millones de usuarias de moda en todo el mundo. Formarás parte de un grupo internacional, trabajando implant (FTE) en el equipo de Ecommerce en un entorno que valora la innovación, la autonomía y el talento, utilizando un stack de datos real y de última generación.

🎯 Tu Misión & Impacto

Como Data analyst te incorporarás al equipo de Ecommerce para transformar datos en insights accionables, modelos analíticos y recomendaciones estratégicas que impulsen la captación, conversión, venta y retención de clientas, así como la reducción de devoluciones.

Trabajarás mano a mano con stakeholders de negocio digital, CRM, paid media y tecnología, asegurando que las decisiones clave se basen en datos y contribuyendo directamente al crecimiento del canal online.

🛠 Responsabilidades


  • Analizar datos de clientes y comportamiento digital para mejorar:Captación, conversión, venta, fidelización y devoluciones.
  • Diseñar, crear y mantener dashboards y reporting en Power BI para los equipos de Ecommerce y negocio.
  • Definir y hacer seguimiento de KPIs clave de Ecommerce (tráfico, conversión, AOV, churn, LTV, etc.) para apoyar la toma de decisiones estratégicas.
  • Integrar y cruzar datos digitales (web/app) con:CRM, paid media y datos transaccionales.
  • Desarrollar modelos analíticos y predictivos orientados a negocio:Segmentación de clientes, scoring, propensión a compra, forecasting de demanda, etc.
  • Velar por la calidad del dato, proponiendo mejoras en procesos y fuentes de información.
  • Preparar y comunicar insights y resultados de manera clara a los distintos stakeholders (Ecommerce, Marketing, Producto, Tech).
  • Colaborar en iniciativas de experimentación digital (A/B testing) para optimizar funnels, campañas y experiencias de usuario.


✅ Requisitos


  • Experiencia previa en entornos Ecommerce (retail, moda, marketplaces o similares).
  • Dominio avanzado de SQL.
  • Experiencia trabajando con BigQuery y Snowflake.
  • Dominio avanzado de Power BI (modelado de datos, DAX, visualizaciones).
  • Nivel avanzado de Excel.
  • Nivel alto de inglés (entorno internacional, documentación y reuniones).


🌟 Se valorará positivamente


  • Experiencia en modelos predictivos:Propensión a compra, churn, LTV, forecasting de demanda, recomendación de productos, etc.
  • Conocimiento de técnicas de machine learning (supervisado y no supervisado).
  • Experiencia con Python (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn).
  • Uso de notebooks (Databricks Notebooks / Jupyter).
  • Experiencia en A/B testing y experimentación digital.
  • Uso de herramientas como GrowthBook y Optimizely.
  • Experiencia trabajando con Azure DevOps y flujos de trabajo colaborativos (versionado, CI/CD de modelos o pipelines).


🧩 Tech Stack

Trabajarás con un stack moderno y orientado a grandes volúmenes de datos:


  • Data & Cloud: BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks
  • Lenguajes: SQL, Python
  • Reporting & BI: Power BI, Excel
  • Experimentación: GrowthBook, Optimizely
  • Colaboración & DevOps: Azure DevOps


🏙 Ubicación & Modelo de trabajo


  • Posición basada en Cerdanyola del Valles, Barcelona, España.
  • Se dispone de autobus de empresa.
  • Presencialidad 2 días a la semana.
  • Entorno multidisciplinar con fuerte foco en negocio digital, datos y tecnología.


🚀 Por qué unirte a Amaris Consulting


  • Crecimiento profesional: el 70% de nuestros líderes senior comenzaron en posiciones junior.
  • Formación continua: acceso a academias internas, formaciones online, webinars y certificaciones patrocinadas por la compañía.
  • Flexibilidad & beneficios: modelo de trabajo híbrido y paquete competitivo de beneficios sociales (a detallar durante el proceso).
  • Bienestar: nuestro programa WeCare te acompaña en situaciones críticas.
  • Desarrollo personal: presupuesto anual de formación para potenciar tus habilidades y acelerar tu carrera.


📅 Fecha de incorporación: ASAP

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.

Take the next step in your career and become a key player in driving change at Amaris Consulting. Apply now to embark on a journey of professional growth and personal fulfillment.

Who are we?

Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1,000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.

Title: Regulatory Reporting Data Analyst
Description:
At ING we are looking for a Regulatory Reporting Data Analyst




A talented and enthusiastic Regulatory Reporting Data Analyst to join our Financial and Regulatory Reporting Team.




The responsibility of this team is handling the external financial and regulatory reporting data delivery to Bank of Spain and Head-Office.

This includes answering ad hoc data requests and maintaining the day-to-day relationship with Head Office. The team also drives continuous improvements in the reporting processes and data quality.

This is a dynamic environment as requirements and expectations of all stakeholders are continuously increasing.

You are a collaborative professional who listens and invest in your coworkers to achieve common goals. You combine strong analytical skills with a natural ability to communicate complex issues in a simple and understandable way to stakeholders.




Your key responsibilities:

As a Regulatory Reporting Data Analyst you will:

Understand the end-to-end Finance processes, data model and architecture, including the interdependencies with other domains.

Keep ongoing development and technical refinement of the data requirements. The objective is to ensure that ING always complies with the reporting requirement from the regulator.

Implement improvement actions during the data processing to optimize the flow to meet the tight deadlines.

Collaborate actively in the different projects involving the reporting area, acting as a technical consultant.

Maintaining a solid control framework to ensure the integrity of ING’s regulatory reports:

- Identify and follow-up the data quality issues on the reporting process including root cause analysis, in close collaboration with the Data Quality Office.

- Perform source data vs. general ledger and cross-report reconciliations.

- Understand and explain main developments in the reports through technical and analytical reviews.




What are we looking for?




Want to know if this job is made for you? These are some of the qualities that we value the most for this role. And if the shoe fits… don’t hesitate to apply!




A strong set of technical and analytical abilities:




- Master’s or bachelor’s degree with 3 to 6 years of experience (preferably in banking and/or consulting).

- Superb analytical skills, being able to analyse large amounts of data and to put outcomes in a wider perspective.

- Proven experience in data analysis, using PLSQL tools and SAS programs.

- Knowledge in the usage of Power BI / Power Query in Excel.

- High level of autonomy and initiative.

- Have a pro-active attitude and ability to operate in a demanding and changing environment.

- Problem solving mindset.

- Excellent speaking and writing skills in English is a must.

- A leader of our Orange Code culture displaying the following behaviors:

- Able to assume personal responsibility.

- You give and ask for constructive feedback.

- Team player that can build bridges across functions and can easily get buy-in from others to get things done within tight deadlines.

- Loving to collaborate with different people and areas across the bank.

- Flexible to be able to adapt fast to the changes.

- Result oriented. Make things happen.

- Strong resilience: ability to navigate through ambiguity.

- Early adopter to new technologies




What do we offer?

The time you spend at work, the challenges you face or the lessons you get are very important, but… What about your personal life? At ING we want your work to fulfill you in every way, and that is why we take care of even the smallest details.




Check out what is waiting for you!




Be flexible my friend.

Our model is all about flexibility and accountability. Keeping both our customers and our colleagues’ needs in mind, you determine together which days you work at home and which you come to ING MAD to offer your best self. Do your thing.

Restaurant card.

So that thinking about what to have for lunch doesn’t take up your time or your cravings.




Our house will be your home.

In our offices you can find electric mobility solutions, doctor, hairdresser, gym, The Good Service (to help you with your errands) and much more!




Health insurance.

For you and all your family (spouse/partner and children).




Life insurance.

We help you protect what matters most to you.




Flexible remuneration.

In addition, you will enjoy our flexible remuneration model, through a more tax-advantaged way, you will be able to access other services such as nursery, transport card, training aids…




Free company shuttle.

It doesn't matter where you live. We have 6 routes (North, Central, South, A5, A42 and A6) to get you to our ING office in Madrid comfortably.




Banking benefits.

Special loans with more beneficial conditions.

Special mortgage conditions after 6 months working at the bank.

And you can benefit from our pension plan after 1 month with us!




More vitality and more belonging

360º care of the employee (physical, mental, social connection & purpose).

Physiotherapy service on site.

Clinics with professional, world-class athletes.

Gympass service.

Employee Networks: Rainbow Lions (LGBTQI+), Leonas & RIÑG.

International Remote work program.

Get to know Us




We are a team of more than 1800 people sharing one idea: doing our bit in helping our clients create a better future for themselves, by providing them with the tools they need to get where they want to go.




A fully online bank where technology, innovation, simplicity and transparency are the main ingredients, and the first one to work with agile methodologies.




And although we are based in Spain, we are lucky to work in an international bank with presence in more than 40 countries.




We are proud to have a diverse team, and we are committed to continue developing a culture of Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging to foster a culture of collaboration and innovation that represents all collectives in the society.




And even though we look orange on the outside, we are green on the inside. Our ambition in Sustainability is Empowering (y)our Sustainability journey. We are embarking on our path, for us, but we are also there for when our clients decide to embark on this journey.




Making things happen, helping others be successful, always being one step ahead—that’s our DNA. If you look at yourself in the mirror and your reflection matches our way of being and working…we are looking forward to meeting you!




At ING, you belong just as you are. We hire and support people for their talent and potential — and we know that when you can be yourself, you do your best work.




Job Application Safety Reminder




We’re seeing an increase in fraudulent job offers. To protect yourself, please follow these key guidelines when applying for roles at ING:

•Apply only via official ING platforms: ING uses Workday and LinkedIn as its internal recruitment system. Applications should be submitted only via our official career site or via ING job postings on LinkedIn.

•Check the sender’s email carefully: Legitimate communication will always come from @ing.com and/or @myworkday.com.

•No payments or banking details will ever be requested: If someone asks for this information, it’s a scam.

Title: Analista de datos
Description:
En ALTEN tenemos claro que el éxito de nuestros proyectos se debe a las personas que forman nuestro equipo. Por eso, si tienes experiencia como Data Analyst, nos comprometemos a impulsar tu talento, satisfacer tus expectativas laborales y hacerte sentir como en casa. ¿Quieres construir hoy el mundo de mañana? ¡Sigue leyendo!







¿Qué buscamos?

Dominio de Power BI para creación de dashboards, reporting y análisis visual.
Conocimientos sólidos de SQL para extracción, transformación y consulta de datos en entornos complejos.
Experiencia con Snowflake en proyectos profesionales.




Competencias valorables:

Conocimiento de SnowQuery.
Familiaridad con Azure Synapse Analytics.
Experiencia con Azure Data Factory.
Conocimientos de Azure Databricks.







¿Qué podemos ofrecerte?

Incorporación a una empresa multinacional competitiva en continuo crecimiento.
Integración en un equipo de profesionales altamente cualificado, con un buen clima laboral, innovador y dinámico.
Formación especializada y desarrollo profesional continuo.
Beneficios Sociales y Plan de Compensación Flexible.
Retribución competitiva.







¿Quiénes somos?

Somos un equipo. Formamos parte de ALTEN, una multinacional europea especializada en proporcionar servicios de consultoría, tecnología de la información y servicios de ingeniería a las principales compañías del mercado español.




Si te gustan las cifras, te diremos que en España tenemos una facturación de 337 millones de euros, 10 sedes y un equipo formado por 5.500 personas.

Title: Analista de Datos con PowerBI
Description:
Empresa líder en el sector de Customer Science y en Marketing Automation, dedicada a la generación de resultados de negocio mediante la gestión avanzada de la información de clientes, liderando cambios del sector y mejora de su posición competitiva, requiere un analista de datos con PowerBI




Funciones Principales

Diseñar y mantener modelos de datos analíticos eficientes.
Crear dashboards e informes interactivos en Power BI.
Desarrollar medidas y cálculos avanzados en DAX.
Integrar y explotar datos desde fuentes cloud y bases de datos.
Participar en arquitecturas analíticas sobre Microsoft Fabric.
Integrar datos mediante ETL y conexiones a APIs y servicios web.
Colaborar con negocio para traducir necesidades en soluciones analíticas claras y accionables.







Requisitos mínimos

✅ Requisitos Mínimos

Imprescindible titulación en Ingeniería Informática o Grado en Ciencia de Datos (se pedirá título).
2 años de experiencia, o más, en TIC.
Experiencia demostrable en: Power BI Desktop & Power BI Service; DAX (Data Analysis Expressions); SQL, T-SQL, PL/SQL; Microsoft Fabric (integración avanzada); Fuentes cloud: Azure Data Lake, SharePoint, OneDrive; Bases de datos: PostgreSQL, Azure SQL Database; ETL: Power Query, Azure Data Factory; Visualización de datos con Qlik View; ; Conexión a APIs y servicios web.
Valorable: Experiencia en entornos cloud y arquitecturas modernas de datos, orientación a negocio y narrativa del dato (data storytelling), capacidad analítica y atención al detalle en diseño visual, experiencia trabajando con grandes volúmenes de información.







¡Si tienes motivación para crecer y transformar datos en decisiones de negocio, te esperamos en nuestro equipo!







Podemos ofrecerte:




Un puesto dinámico en una compañía con ganas de crecer, desarrollarse y conseguir más éxitos gracias a tu ayuda. Además:




📄 Contrato indefinido
🚀 Carrera profesional: ¡tú decides hasta dónde quieres llegar!
📚 Formación: udemy, LKD learning, plataformas de idiomas...
😊 Buen ambiente laboral
⏰ Flexibilidad horaria
🕒 Horario: jornada completa
🏠 Teletrabajo híbrido (reuniones puntuales presenciales en Madrid).

¿Quieres conocer más? ¡Ínscribete!

Description:

El equipo

Formarás parte de un equipo de datos completo y especializado, estructurado en dos grandes áreas:

Data Analytics (área en la que se incorporará esta posición)
Data Engineering & Architecture

Incidencias
Cambios

Analítica avanzada, especialmente modelos predictivos basados en Machine Learning.
Gobernanza del dato, con especial foco en el concepto de ontologías.
Proyectos co‑creados con el negocio, priorizando enfoques de consultoría y acompañamiento continuo frente a proyectos cerrados y estáticos.




Responsabilidades adicionales

Aprendizaje continuo de las herramientas, procesos y servicios internos sobre los que se da soporte
Propuestas de mejora continua del catálogo de productos y servicios del equipo
Impartición de formación a grupos de clientes internos cuando el servicio lo requiera.
Relación directa con unidades de negocio, clientes internos y otros equipos de la organización.

Power Query

Microsoft Fabric

Title: Data Analyst - Revenue and Market Analytics
Description:
What You'll Do:

As part of the Revenue and Market Analytics team within Revenue Operations, your main mission will be to deliver robust, intuitive, and consumable business insights to empower Criteo’s Global Commercial leaders to build and drive their commercial strategy.

You will implement a data-driven approach to uncover insights and explain business performance. This role involves working with large and complex datasets, and requires someone who is comfortable exploring data creatively to identify trends, generate insights, and build a compelling narrative that informs decision-making.

Your Mission


Provide analytical insights on Criteo’s global performance, market dynamics, and competitive landscape
Identify business growth opportunities and threats, and translate findings into actionable insights for C‑level stakeholders
Leverage external and internal data to assess the potential of clients and prospects across new and existing business



Who You Are:


Excellent communication skills and fluency in English
2–3 years of experience in a similar analytics role (experience in AdTech is a plus)
Bachelor’s degree or higher in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, Business School)
Hands-on experience with SQL for data extraction/manipulation; proficiency with Python for data analysis, and experience with visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI
Curiosity and experience using Generative AI solutions to enhance data exploration, automate insights, or craft analytical narratives
Strong intellectual curiosity and analytical mindset; ability to structure and solve complex problems; solid understanding of statistical modelling
Business acumen, genuine interest in the ad tech industry and ability to act as a trusted advisor to commercial stakeholders
Strong presentation skills (including PowerPoint proficiency) and ability to translate data into clear business insights and recommendations
A proactive, autonomous mindset, with the ability to manage multiple projects, set milestones, coordinate with cross-functional teams, and drive initiatives forward



We acknowledge that many candidates may not meet every single role requirement listed above. If your experience looks a little different from our requirements but you believe that you can still bring value to the role, we’d love to see your application!

Who We Are:

We’re Criteo, the Commerce Intelligence Platform. Criteo helps businesses turn shopper signals into commerce outcomes while delivering more relevant experiences for shoppers. We use proprietary commerce intelligence and AI decisioning to drive relevance for shoppers and performance for businesses.

At Criteo, our culture is as unique as it is diverse. From our offices across the globe or from the comfort of home, our 3,600 Criteos collaborate together to build an open, impactful, and forward-thinking environment.

We foster a workplace where everyone is valued, and employment decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and business needs—never on non-job-related factors or legally protected characteristics.

What We Offer:

🏢 Ways of working – Our hybrid model blends home with in-office experiences, making space for both.

📈 Grow with us – Learning, mentorship & career development programs.

💪 Your wellbeing matters – Health benefits, wellness perks & mental health support.

🤝 A team that cares – Diverse, inclusive, and globally connected.

💸 Fair pay & perks – Attractive salary, with performance-based rewards and family-friendly policies, plus the potential for equity depending on role and level.

Additional benefits may vary depending on the country where you work and the nature of your employment with Criteo.

Title: DPO Analista de Datos
Place: Villanueva de la Cañada, Community of Madrid, Spain
Description:
La Universidad Camilo José Cela busca incorporar a un/a Analista de Datos.

Funciones


Analizar datos de negocio para identificar tendencias, desviaciones, oportunidades y áreas de mejora.
Extraer, depurar, transformar y estructurar datos procedentes de distintas fuentes internas y externas.
Diseñar, elaborar, actualizar y mantener informes, dashboards y cuadros de mando para el seguimiento de KPIs.
Interpretar resultados y convertir los datos en recomendaciones útiles para la toma de decisiones.
Dar soporte analítico a los equipos de marketing y promoción en el seguimiento de campañas, acciones comerciales, rendimiento de iniciativas y evolución de indicadores.
Definir, revisar y dar seguimiento a métricas clave del negocio junto con las áreas implicadas.
Realizar análisis recurrentes y ad hoc para responder a necesidades concretas del negocio.
Colaborar con equipos de marketing y promoción para entender necesidades, priorizar requerimientos y traducirlos en soluciones analíticas.
Trabajar transversalmente con TIC / Tecnología, Gestión de Datos y otros departamentos para asegurar la disponibilidad, calidad, integración y correcta explotación de la información.
Asegurar la consistencia, fiabilidad y calidad de los datos utilizados en el reporting y en los análisis.
Identificar oportunidades de automatización de procesos de análisis y reporting para mejorar la eficiencia operativa.
Apoyar la evolución de la cultura de datos en la organización, facilitando una visión más analítica y orientada a evidencia dentro del negocio.
Colaborar en la mejora de procesos, herramientas y metodologías relacionadas con el uso del dato.
Preparar presentaciones e informes ejecutivos para compartir conclusiones con áreas de negocio y otros interlocutores internos.


Requisitos


Formación universitaria (preferiblemente en ramas técnicas, matemáticas o ingenierías).
Experiencia mínima de 3 años en posiciones similares.
Conocimientos sólidos de análisis de datos y analítica empresarial.
Manejo de SQL para consulta, extracción y tratamiento de datos.
Manejo de Python para análisis, tratamiento y automatización de procesos.
Experiencia con herramientas de visualización y reporting, especialmente Microsoft Power BI.
Conocimientos de estadística aplicada al análisis de datos.
Conocimientos de minería de datos.
Familiaridad con entornos y conceptos de Big Data.
Experiencia o familiaridad con Salesforce como herramienta de CRM.
Manejo habitual de herramientas de Microsoft Office, especialmente Excel y PowerPoint.
Capacidad para interpretar información compleja y traducirla en conclusiones claras para negocio.
Capacidad para trabajar con múltiples fuentes de información y estructurarlas de forma útil y accionable.


Se Ofrece


Fecha de incorporación: mayo de 2026.
Contrato indefinido.
Jornada completa.
Ubicación: Campus Villafranca (Madrid).

📍 Sant Cugat del Vallès (Híbrido)




Buscamos un/a Data Analyst para incorporarse a un proyecto estratégico de Gobierno del Dato, donde tendrás un papel clave en la organización, calidad y documentación de la información, trabajando en un entorno dinámico y colaborativo.




🎯 ¿Cuál será tu misión?




Formarás parte del equipo de Data Governance & Management, apoyando la implementación de un Catálogo de Datos y la mejora de la calidad, trazabilidad y uso de la información en la organización.

Apoyar en la identificación y documentación de Elementos de Datos Críticos (CDEs).

Aplicar y actualizar reglas de clasificación y calidad del dato.

Colaborar con distintos equipos para asegurar el correcto uso y entendimiento de los datos.

Experiencia de 3 años en análisis de datos o roles similares.
Conocimientos básicos/medios de SQL, Python y análisis de datos.
Interés por el Gobierno del Dato, calidad del dato y documentación.
Familiaridad (o interés) en herramientas de Data Catalog / Business Glossary / Data Lineage (Purview, Collibra, Alation, etc.).
Perfil analítico, detallista, con ganas de aprender y buena capacidad de comunicación.

Participar en un proyecto estratégico de Data Governance.
Entorno de trabajo híbrido y colaborativo.
Oportunidad real de aprendizaje y desarrollo profesional en datos.
Acompañamiento y mentoría por parte de perfiles senior.

Title: Fraud Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Satispay began by rethinking the simple act of a payment to remove the friction from our daily routines. But we didn't stop there. Today, we are building a complete financial platform designed to empower people and concretely improve their lives.

By giving our 6 million users a clear, open path to pay, save, and invest, we are evolving into the definitive destination for every financial need.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Fraud Data Analyst, you'll be the person who secures the future of digital payments by architecting sophisticated real-time defense strategies.

Here's What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like


Architect Real-Time Strategies – You will be responsible for our real-time decisioning engine, designing and deploying logic that combines ML scores with velocity features to make millisecond decisions.
Precision Rule Development – Translate complex fraud patterns into high-performing rules, optimizing the balance between False Positive Rates (FPR) and Gross Fraud Loss.
Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.


Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?


Experience – You have between 3 and 7 years of professional experience, specifically within the risk, payments, or credit sectors.
Technical Proficiency – You are highly skilled in SQL and Python for data analysis and strategy development.
Domain Expertise – You have a solid understanding of payment fraud dynamics and experience working with real-time decisioning environments.
Communication – You possess excellent communication skills and are fluent in English.
Nice to Have – Experience with Machine Learning, Data Science, PySpark, or GraphQL knowledge is a significant plus.


CareAbout: how we support your impact

About

We move fast, and evolution never stops. It's a fun ride, but it can be challenging. To make sure our people truly thrive, we're committed to making their lives easier, both in the office and out in the world. That's why we created CareAbout:


❤️ Health (Private insurance for you and your family, psychological support with Serenis, mental health workshops)
💰 Financial resources (Stock Option Plan, Meal vouchers, Relocation support if you're moving countries)
⚙️ Growth and development (Professional development programs, Internal mobility, Language courses with Preply)
🌱 Flexibility (Unlimited PTO, Hybrid working policy*, Flexible working hours)
👨‍👩‍👧 Family (Enhanced parental leave, Additional leave for child sickness)
We embrace three days per week in-office (Tuesday and Thursday + 1 of your choice), with the option to request extra remote time.


Equal Opportunity Employer

At Satispay, we're proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and inclusion, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds. This opportunity is open to everyone, regardless - for instance - of race, colour, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, and national origin. Join us in a workplace where everyone belongs!

Learn more about us

Our values and pillars aren't just fancy words on a page - they really shape everything we do. Explore them here.

By submitting this application, I acknowledge that I have read and understood the content of the Privacy Policy

  • Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
  • Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
  • Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
  • Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
  • Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.

Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?

  • Experience – You have between 3 and 7 years of professional experience, specifically within the risk, payments, or credit sectors.
  • Technical Proficiency – You are highly skilled in SQL and Python for data analysis and strategy development.
  • Domain Expertise – You have a solid understanding of payment fraud dynamics and experience working with real-time decisioning environments.
  • Communication – You possess excellent communication skills and are fluent in English.
  • Nice to Have – Experience with Machine Learning, Data Science, PySpark, or GraphQL knowledge is a significant plus.

CareAbout: how we support your impact

About

We move fast, and evolution never stops. It's a fun ride, but it can be challenging. To make sure our people truly thrive, we're committed to making their lives easier, both in the office and out in the world. That's why we created CareAbout:

  • ❤️ Health (Private insurance for you and your family, psychological support with Serenis, mental health workshops)
  • 💰 Financial resources (Stock Option Plan, Meal vouchers, Relocation support if you're moving countries)
  • ⚙️ Growth and development (Professional development programs, Internal mobility, Language courses with Preply)
  • 🌱 Flexibility (Unlimited PTO, Hybrid working policy*, Flexible working hours)
  • 👨‍👩‍👧 Family (Enhanced parental leave, Additional leave for child sickness)
  • We embrace three days per week in-office (Tuesday and Thursday + 1 of your choice), with the option to request extra remote time.

Equal Opportunity Employer

At Satispay, we're proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and inclusion, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds. This opportunity is open to everyone, regardless - for instance - of race, colour, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, and national origin. Join us in a workplace where everyone belongs!

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
About Us

With over 185 years of history, we are a leading health and wellness company. Our portfolio includes iconic brands such as Aquilea, Fisiocrem, Laborest, Sidroga, Biodramina, Aerored, Fave de Fuca, Halibut, Ems, and Depuralina, with a strong presence across major European markets.

We believe in the power of nature, enhanced by science, as the best way to improve human health and wellbeing sustainably. Our goal is to create a positive impact on both people's lives and the planet. We are currently seeking a highly motivated and skilled Junior Data Analyst to join our team.

Your Mission

We are currently looking to strengthen our Data team by appointing a Junior Data Analyst who will support the development of reporting and data visualization solutions across key areas of the business. As part of the Data Department, you will work closely with senior data profiles and business stakeholders, acting as a bridge between data and decision-making. You will contribute to transforming data into clear, reliable, and actionable insights, supporting a data-driven culture in an international and dynamic environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

In this role, you will be expected to:


Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports in Power BI for different business areas (e.g., sales, operations, finance)
Gather and understand reporting requirements from business users, translating needs into clear and insightful visualizations
Perform exploratory data analysis to identify trends, anomalies, and improvement opportunities
Ensure data quality and consistency across reports by identifying and addressing discrepancies
Collaborate with Data Engineering teams to support the development of optimized data models for reporting
Support the automation of recurring reports and assist users in understanding and using dashboards effectively


Sector: Industria farmacéutica

Función: Ingeniería / Proyectos

Dirige a otros: No

Nivel profesional: Empleado nivel inicial

You'll excel if you have...


1-2 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or similar roles (relevant internships are also considered).
Solid hands-on experience with Power BI (dashboard creation, data modeling basics, DAX, Power Query), along with strong SQL skills for data extraction and transformation, and advanced Excel capabilities
Good understanding of data modeling concepts (e.g., star schema, table relationships) and experience working with structured data sources
Strong analytical mindset with high attention to detail, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and reliability of data and reporting outputs
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, translating business needs into actionable insights
Proactive, organized, and eager to learn, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment
Familiarity with additional BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Qlik), cloud environments (Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks), or business systems such as SAP, Salesforce, or Odoo will be considered a plus

Title: Risk Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 Shape the Future of Digital Commerce with Your Visionary Expertise 🌐







About Miravia

Miravia is a cutting-edge e-commerce platform connecting buyers and sellers seamlessly. We pride ourselves on offering a diverse and dynamic shopping experience by bringing on board innovative brands across various categories. Located in Madrid, we work with teams from all over the world to build the e-commerce platforms of tomorrow.




The Role: Risk Data Analyst

As a Risk Data Analyst, you will play a key role in protecting the organization from financial, operational, and strategic risks. You will serve as the technical bridge between raw data and actionable business intelligence — analyzing complex datasets to identify potential threats, assess their impact, and enable data-driven decision-making across the company. This position is essential for proactively managing risk and preventing losses.




Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis

Execute company-wide data strategies by analyzing diverse datasets, including financial statements, operational logs, and market trends. Identify existing risks, detect emerging threats, and uncover patterns of financial crime. Your analysis will be critical in identifying areas where the business may face financial loss or miss strategic opportunities.

Impact Modelling

Develop and maintain advanced risk models to support forecasting and scenario analysis. Quantify the potential financial impact of business decisions or external events, enabling the Chief Risk Officer (CRO) team to evaluate risk appetite and make informed strategic choices.

Dashboards and Reporting

Prepare and present detailed reports and interactive dashboards for the CRO, senior management, and key stakeholders. Visualize key risk indicators, trends, and alerts to provide a clear overview of the company’s risk landscape and support ongoing monitoring of our risk management framework.

Tool Integration and Automation

Enhance our technical risk infrastructure by integrating risk management systems, supporting data workflows, and building automated solutions for data collection, analysis, and reporting. Help ensure our risk detection capabilities are efficient, scalable, and robust.

Transaction Monitoring

Conduct real-time analysis of financial transactions to identify and investigate suspicious activity. Serve as a frontline defense against fraud and financial loss, while generating insights that inform broader risk assessments and model development.




Requirements

Intermediate proficiency in SQL for data querying, manipulation, and analysis
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with an investigative mindset and excellent attention to detail
Proactive learner with a strong interest in developing expertise in fraud detection, risk management, and data analytics
Comfortable working with large and complex datasets
Experience with programming languages such as Python or R for data analysis and automation
Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau or similar)
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics), or equivalent practical experience
Prior exposure to, or strong interest in, fraud prevention, trust & safety, or risk-scoring systems







Application Process:




Ready to embark on this journey with us? Please send your CV in English!




Please note this role requires to work 100% from the office. Our offices are very centric tough: Metro Station Avenida de América 😃 (Madrid).




Whether you tick every box or bring a unique blend of skills, we want to hear from you. Diversity is our strength, and we encourage applications from those who bring distinct perspectives to our team.




Join Miravia, and let's shape the e-commerce landscape together!

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Company Description 

Ona Therapeutics is a biotech company located in Barcelona (Parc Científic de Barcelona, PCB), focused on developing first-in-class antibody drug conjugates (ADCs) for advanced cancer treatment. By partnering with leading academics and clinicians, we identify novel therapeutic targets from the unique biology of treatment refractory advanced cancer patients, to provide effective new treatments for patients with no remaining alternatives. 

 

Scope 

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst with a strong scientific background to support data-driven decision-making across our project portfolio and strategic initiatives. 




This role sits at the intersection of science, data, and project strategy, supporting the Head of Project & Business Management in transforming complex biological, clinical, and industry data into structured insights that guide internal decision-making. 




You will play a key role in strengthening how the company uses data to evaluate priorities, track programs, and understand the competitive landscape. 

 

Role and Responsibilities  

Business Intelligence & Data Analysis  

Develop a deep understanding of the biotech/pharma landscape, including competitors, modalities, and therapeutic trends 
Gather, structure, and analyse scientific, clinical, and regulatory data (clinical trials, publications, databases, etc.) 
Translate complex datasets into clear insights, summaries, and decision-support materials 
Support portfolio and project teams by providing data-driven insights for prioritisation and planning 
Contribute to the definition of key analytical frameworks and indicators for project and portfolio evaluation 
Support the Head of Project & Business Management in preparing data-driven analyses for internal decision-making 




Strategic Insight & External Analysis 

Support evaluation of external opportunities (assets, indications, collaborations) through structured data analysis 
Contribute to competitive intelligence and landscape mapping 
Assist in preparing analytical inputs for strategic discussions and presentations 




Scientific Integration 

Participate in internal scientific and project meetings 
Collaborate closely with R&D and project management teams to understand data needs 
Contribute to a data-driven culture across project execution and decision-making 




Tools & Data Systems 

Support the structuring and maintenance of internal BI and project intelligence databases 
Work with scientific databases, literature sources, and internal data systems 
Contribute to improving how data is collected, structured, and visualised across projects 

 

Qualifications Requirements  

PhD in relevant scientific fields (Biology, Biochemistry, Pharmacology, Biomedical Sciences, etc.) 
Relevant professional experience in business intelligence highly analytical roles within biotech, consulting, venture capital, or technology transfer, demonstrating strong ability to structure complex problems, interpret data-driven insights, and support evidence-based decision-making. 
Strong interest in biotech, data analysis, and project/portfolio decision-making 
Experience in oncology, immunology, drug-development or related 

 

Skills 

Strong analytical, structured, and systematic mindset  
Excellent critical thinking and problem-solving abilities  
Data-driven with a hypothesis-oriented approach  
Ability to translate complex scientific and clinical data into clear, actionable insights  
Ability to simplify complex information and extract key messages  
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills  
Excellent communication and presentation skills  
Intellectual curiosity and proactive mindset  
Ownership mindset with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment

﻿ 

What we offer 

A work environment, culture and passionate team in the oncology sector. 
A unique opportunity to work in an innovative and dynamic environment. 
Internal benefits such as flexible remuneration, flexible working hours, events along the year... 

 

To apply, please submit your CV and cover letter to hr@ona-therapeutics.com 

We look forward to reviewing your application and discovering how your skills and experience align with our company’s needs.

Company Description 

Ona Therapeutics is a biotech company located in Barcelona (Parc Científic de Barcelona, PCB), focused on developing first-in-class antibody drug conjugates (ADCs) for advanced cancer treatment. By partnering with leading academics and clinicians, we identify novel therapeutic targets from the unique biology of treatment refractory advanced cancer patients, to provide effective new treatments for patients with no remaining alternatives. 

 

Scope 

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst with a strong scientific background to support data-driven decision-making across our project portfolio and strategic initiatives. 


This role sits at the intersection of science, data, and project strategy, supporting the Head of Project & Business Management in transforming complex biological, clinical, and industry data into structured insights that guide internal decision-making. 


You will play a key role in strengthening how the company uses data to evaluate priorities, track programs, and understand the competitive landscape. 

 

Role and Responsibilities  

Business Intelligence & Data Analysis  

  • Develop a deep understanding of the biotech/pharma landscape, including competitors, modalities, and therapeutic trends 
  • Gather, structure, and analyse scientific, clinical, and regulatory data (clinical trials, publications, databases, etc.) 
  • Translate complex datasets into clear insights, summaries, and decision-support materials 
  • Support portfolio and project teams by providing data-driven insights for prioritisation and planning 
  • Contribute to the definition of key analytical frameworks and indicators for project and portfolio evaluation 
  • Support the Head of Project & Business Management in preparing data-driven analyses for internal decision-making 


Strategic Insight & External Analysis 

  • Support evaluation of external opportunities (assets, indications, collaborations) through structured data analysis 
  • Contribute to competitive intelligence and landscape mapping 
  • Assist in preparing analytical inputs for strategic discussions and presentations 


Scientific Integration 

  • Participate in internal scientific and project meetings 
  • Collaborate closely with R&D and project management teams to understand data needs 
  • Contribute to a data-driven culture across project execution and decision-making 


Tools & Data Systems 

  • Support the structuring and maintenance of internal BI and project intelligence databases 
  • Work with scientific databases, literature sources, and internal data systems 
  • Contribute to improving how data is collected, structured, and visualised across projects 

 

Qualifications Requirements  

  • PhD in relevant scientific fields (Biology, Biochemistry, Pharmacology, Biomedical Sciences, etc.) 
  • Relevant professional experience in business intelligence highly analytical roles within biotech, consulting, venture capital, or technology transfer, demonstrating strong ability to structure complex problems, interpret data-driven insights, and support evidence-based decision-making. 
  • Strong interest in biotech, data analysis, and project/portfolio decision-making 
  • Experience in oncology, immunology, drug-development or related 

 

Skills 

  • Strong analytical, structured, and systematic mindset  
  • Excellent critical thinking and problem-solving abilities  
  • Data-driven with a hypothesis-oriented approach  
  • Ability to translate complex scientific and clinical data into clear, actionable insights  
  • Ability to simplify complex information and extract key messages  
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills  
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills  
  • Intellectual curiosity and proactive mindset  
  • Ownership mindset with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment

﻿ 

What we offer 

  • A work environment, culture and passionate team in the oncology sector. 
  • A unique opportunity to work in an innovative and dynamic environment. 
  • Internal benefits such as flexible remuneration, flexible working hours, events along the year... 

 

To apply, please submit your CV and cover letter to hr@ona-therapeutics.com 

We look forward to reviewing your application and discovering how your skills and experience align with our company’s needs.

Title: Data Analytics Consultant (Power BI & Python)
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
En BETWEEN apostamos por ofrecerte las mejores oportunidades dentro del área tecnológica. Contamos con un equipo de más de 950 profesionales. Ser parte de nuestro equipo significa formar parte de una comunidad multicultural, con acceso a oportunidades de crecimiento y desarrollo profesional, generando impacto.

Buscamos un Consultor/a - Big Data - Data Analytics con una mentalidad analítica y orientación práctica para integrarse a nuestros equipos. Tu función principal será el soporte de datos, la visualización y el reporting, trabajando de forma autónoma en tareas operativas y técnicas con un fuerte interés por la automatización y la mejora continua de procesos.

¿Qué haré?


Creación y mantenimiento de dashboards e informes en Power BI.
Extracción, validación y análisis de datos mediante SQL.
Soporte en automatización de tareas y tratamiento de datos con Python.
Generación de reportes periódicos, seguimiento de KPIs y control de calidad de datos.
Coordinación con equipos internos para entender necesidades de datos, documentación de procesos y gestión de bases de datos.


¿Cómo lo haré?


Tendrás horario flexible.
Contarás con un PC y los periféricos necesarios para tu trabajo.
2 días oficina, 3 días desde casa


Beneficios De Trabajar Con Nosotros En BETWEEN

🩺 Cuidado y bienestar: Seguro médico con Sanitas.

📚 Formación y desarrollo: Idiomas, Udemy, certificaciones, The Power Business School, y bootcamps especializados (Agile, DevOps, IA & Big Data, Ciberseguridad, etc.).

☕ Acompañamiento personalizado: Equipo de Talento a tu disposición y cafés mensuales para mantener el contacto.

💼 Proyectos desafiantes: Retos alineados con tus objetivos personales y profesionales.

💰 Flexibilidad salarial: Ventajas fiscales con Retribución Flexible (restauración, transporte, guardería).

🏋️ Vida activa: Descuentos en Gympass para fitness.

🛍️ Descuentos exclusivos: Acceso al Club Inspiring Benefits.

🤝 Networking y eventos: BETWEEN Nights, torneos deportivos, viernes de vermut, juegos de mesa y más.

🏢 Oficinas espectaculares: En el 22@, con rooftop, tirador de cerveza, ping-pong y futbolín.

💵 Recompensa por recomendar talento: Programa de recomendación remunerado.

En BETWEEN, la igualdad de oportunidades es uno de nuestros valores. Nuestro compromiso es contratar al mejor talento independientemente de su raza, religión, sexo, edad y personas con otras capacidades y promover su desarrollo profesional y personal.

Requisitos


Experiencia previa en posiciones relacionadas con datos, reporting o análisis.
Conocimientos de SQL para consultas y extracción de información.
Experiencia de Python para manipulación o automatización de datos.
Experiencia trabajando con Power BI, visualización de datos y buen manejo de Excel.
Nivel de inglés avanzado.
Conocimiento en modelado de datos, grandes volúmenes de información y procesos ETL.

Contrato indefinido: 📄

Jornada intensiva todos los viernes y el mes de agosto: 🗓️

Disfruta de jornadas intensivas los viernes y durante todo el mes de agosto para optimizar tu tiempo libre y equilibrar tu vida laboral."

Metodología SmartWorking: 🌐

Nos adaptamos a tu estilo de vida con nuestra metodología SmartWorking, que fomenta la flexibilidad y el teletrabajo, permitiéndote alcanzar un equilibrio entre tu carrera y tus compromisos personales.




Oficinas Wellbeing: 🎾☕

En nuestra sede de la Agencia West, contamos con instalaciones de bienestar de las que podrás disfrutar: gimnasio equipado, pista de pádel, rocódromo, clases individuales… Además, disponemos de Coffee Corner con café de especialidad para que puedas relajarte y disfrutar de tu pausa.




IT&Engineering Academy: 🎓

Además contamos con licencias de Coursera, formación AdHoc e idiomas. 📚




Oportunidad de aprender de grandes profesionales: 👨 🏫

Aprovecha la oportunidad de aprender de los mejores. Nuestros programas de coaching y mentoring te brindan la guía y el apoyo necesarios para alcanzar tu máximo potencial.

Tenemos Convenios con distintas Asociaciones y Colegios de Ingenieros, brindándote la

posibilidad de acceder a formaciones y normativa específica. 📝




Proyección de futuro y estabilidad: 🚀

Diseña tu propio camino hacia el éxito con nuestro plan de carrera personalizado, que te ofrece proyección de futuro y estabilidad a largo plazo en un ambiente de trabajo colaborativo y motivador.




Movilidad geográfica: 🌍

Explora nuevas oportunidades y experiencias internacionales con nuestra opción de movilidad geográfica, permitiéndote expandir tus horizontes tanto profesional como personalmente.

Eventos de empresa y networking: 🤝




Forma parte de una comunidad cercana y vibrante.

Deseamos atraer a los profesionales más destacados y, para lograrlo, te incentivamos a que nos refieras a aquellos talentos excepcionales que conozcas.




Plan de rebranding: 🔄

Forma parte de nuestros embajadores de marca en redes sociales, contribuyendo a la estrategia de rebranding de la empresa, así como optando a distintos premios.

Únete a un equipo donde se valora tu bienestar y se promueve un ambiente inclusivo y positivo. ¡Somos un Great Place to Work reconocido por nuestro excepcional clima laboral!

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
Descripción de la oferta

En nuestro Programa Talento Joven buscamos personas recién tituladas en Formación Profesional de informática, análisis de datos o programación, con interés en adquirir experiencia y conocimientos prácticos en el campo del Data Science.










Funciones:

Participar en un programa formativo de 30 semanas enfocado en habilidades técnicas y prácticas de análisis de datos.
Trabajar sobre proyectos reales y simulaciones del entorno empresarial, incluyendo retos de predicción, análisis exploratorio y visualización de datos.
Colaborar y aprender directamente de profesionales en activo que te acompañarán durante tu evolución.
Practicar con herramientas como Python, SQL, Pandas, Power BI, Jupyter Notebooks y Scikit-learn.
Aplicar procedimientos y buenas prácticas de análisis en entornos controlados de laboratorio.







Lo que ofrecemos:

Plan formativo diseñado y actualizado según las necesidades reales del mercado laboral en data.
Formación práctica, en grupo reducido, orientada al trabajo que se realiza en equipos de data science.
Mentoring personalizado con especialistas del sector.
Posibilidad real de entrevista para ofertas de empleo al finalizar el programa en empresas colaboradoras.
Red de contacto con profesionales y exalumnos del programa.







Requisitos:

Ciclo Formativo de Grado Medio o Superior relacionado con informática, análisis de datos o programación.
Interés por el análisis de datos y motivación para afrontar retos prácticos.
Capacidad para trabajar en equipo, comunicarte y aprender en un entorno técnico.







Tipo de puesto: Formativo/Prácticas

Ubicación: Modalidad híbrida (presencial y online) según disponibilidad.

Duración: 30 semanas.

Incorporación: Octubre 2026.

  • Interés por el análisis de datos y motivación para afrontar retos prácticos.
  • Capacidad para trabajar en equipo, comunicarte y aprender en un entorno técnico.



Tipo de puesto: Formativo/Prácticas

Ubicación: Modalidad híbrida (presencial y online) según disponibilidad.

Duración: 30 semanas.

Incorporación: Octubre 2026.

Title: Analista de datos
Description:
Desde Eurofirms se busca incorporar un/a Analista de Reporting y Bases de Datos para integrarse en un entorno corporativo estable y consolidado en una gran empresa situada en Barajas.




Funciones principales

Gestión y soporte de aplicaciones internas.

Análisis de nuevas necesidades funcionales y apoyo en la mejora de procesos.

Revisión y resolución de incidencias en distintos módulos de programa.

Mantenimiento de herramientas internas de seguimiento y control de indicadores.

Elaboración de reporting y automatización de informes.

Mantenimiento y explotación de bases de datos SQL.




Requisitos

Estar actualmente en situación de desempleo e inscrito/a como demandante de empleo en el SEPE.

Experiencia previa en un puesto de características similares.

Nivel avanzado o experto de Excel y Access.

Conocimientos de Power BI.

Experiencia sólida trabajando con bases de datos y lenguaje SQL, orientada a la extracción y generación de informes.

Experiencia en tratamiento y análisis de datos (data mining).

Conocimientos de scripting en entornos Windows (VBScript, archivos .bat/.cmd y páginas ASP).

Experiencia con SQL en entornos Oracle, Informix, DB2 y SQL Server.

Manejo de ficheros EDI (EDIFACT/XML).

Alto nivel de capacidad analítica, atención al detalle y autonomía en el trabajo.




Condiciones

Contrato indefinido de relevo.

Jornada completa (40 horas semanales).

Puesto de trabajo ubicado en Madrid.

Rango salarial orientativo: entre 35.000 y 38.000 € brutos anuales, en función de la experiencia aportada.

Title: Controller de Gestión / Data Analyst
Description:
En estos momentos, estamos buscando para nuestro cliente del sector agro un/a Controller de Gestión y Procesos, con una clara visión de negocio, capaz de entender nuestras operaciones en detalle y utilizar el análisis de datos como palanca para mejorar la eficiencia y la toma de decisiones.

FUNCIONES Y TAREAS

Buscamos un profesional tecno-funcional que actuará como nexo estratégico entre las áreas operativas (compras, ventas, producción agrícola y logística) y la tecnología, consolidando el sistema ERP e impulsando una cultura orientada al dato (Business Intelligence).

REQUISITOS


Experiencia en herramientas de Business Intelligence y análisis/cruce de datos
Capacidad analítica y orientación a resultados
Control de gestión
Habilidades de comunicación y empatía para interactuar con distintos departamentos
Mentalidad resolutiva, proactiva y con tolerancia a entornos exigentes


¿Qué ofrece la empresa?


Estabilidad laboral (contrato indefinido)
Salario a valorar en función de la experiencia
Flexibilidad
Posibilidad puntual de teletrabajo (aunque el puesto es principalmente presencial)
Retribución abierta según experiencia del candidato
Ubicación: Cercano a Cuevas de Almanzora


Reclut es una consultora boutique de reclutamiento y headhunting. Ofrece un servicios personalizado y adaptado a las necesidades de cada cliente.

Su propósito es ayudar a las empresas a encontrar el mejor talento, así como ofrecer nuevas oportunidades para las personas en búsqueda de empleo.

Title: IT Support Specialist
Description:
Who We Are



Cint is a pioneer in research technology (ResTech). Our customers use the Cint platform to post questions and get answers from real people to build business strategies, confidently publish research, accurately measure the impact of digital advertising, and more. The Cint platform is built on a programmatic marketplace, which is the world’s largest, with nearly 300 million respondents in over 150 countries who consent to sharing their opinions, motivations, and behaviours.



We are feeding the world’s curiosity!





The Role



In this role as an IT Support Specialist, your impact extends far beyond traditional hardware and software troubleshooting. You will serve as the primary lead for EU Inventory Management, ensuring a seamless onboarding experience through the precise provisioning and global shipment of hardware. Additionally, you will play a key role in supporting our Barcelona office infrastructure. As a forward-thinking member of the team, you will help provide the best technical experience for our global workforce.





The Team



The IT Support team at Cint is a globally distributed, fast-paced group dedicated to providing seamless technology experiences. We are the backbone that enables our global employees to work securely and efficiently, whether they are in our offices, at home or travelling. We challenge the status quo by always looking for how we can make our service offering smarter, faster and give more value for our users.





Job Description



Provide technical support and assistance to end-users, both remotely and on-site, by diagnosing and resolving complex hardware, software (including Windows & Mac OS), and network issues.



Manage IT Asset stock for Europe in line with request trends and coordinate with logistics to ship and retrieve assets in a timely manner.



Maintain office equipment like ZoomRooms and Printers etc & office internet, and ensure these are working flawlessly without interruption



Manage and prioritise multiple support requests, ensuring adherence to service level agreements (SLAs) and delivering exceptional customer service.



Participate in IT projects and initiatives and Assist in the support, deployment, configuration, and maintenance of IT infrastructure, network devices, and workstations.



Maintain accurate records of support activities, including incident reports, resolutions, and technical procedures documentation.



Provide technical knowledge around our internal systems & record videos on trending issues periodically



Document procedures and solutions, and constantly maintain and improve the knowledge base, both for IT internal use and for the Cint employees.





Qualifications



Must Have:



Solid experience in providing technical support, with a focus on troubleshooting IT issues.



Experience with a ticketing system (Service Cloud, Zendesk, Jira, etc.)



Good understanding of Windows & Mac OS systems, networking, hardware and software.



A genuine interest in IT, technology and doing things systematically. A proactive approach, a talent for troubleshooting and a great collaborator.



Nice to have:



Being familiar with tools and environments like Google, Slack, Okta, Atlassian, MS O365 and MDM (Intune preferred).



Basic knowledge of ITIL processes (Incident, Service Request and Change Management) and IT Security.



Ability to work independently, prioritise tasks, and maintain attention to detail in a fast-paced environment.





Additional Information



This position is a full-time position with a hybrid model, meaning minimum 3 days in the office.





Our Values



Collaboration is our superpower



We uncover rich perspectives across the world
Success happens together
We deliver across borders.

Innovation is in our blood



We’re pioneers in our industry
Our curiosity is insatiable
We bring the best ideas to life.

We do what we say



We’re accountable for our work and actions
Excellence comes as standard
We’re open, honest and kind, always.

We are caring



We learn from each other’s experiences
Stop and listen; every opinion matters
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion.





More About Cint



We’re proud to be recognised in Newsweek’s 2025 Global Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces®, reflecting our commitment to a culture of trust, respect, and employee growth.



In June 2021, Cint acquired Berlin-based GapFish – the world’s largest ISO certified online panel community in the DACH region – and in January 2022, completed the acquisition of US-based Lucid – a programmatic research technology platform that provides access to first-party survey data in over 110 countries.



Cint Group AB (publ), listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, this growth has made Cint a strong global platform with teams across its many global offices, including Stockholm, London, New York, New Orleans, Singapore, Tokyo and Sydney. (www.cint.com)







Additionally, in a world of AI, we want our candidates to understand our approach to the use of AI during the interview and hiring process, so we'd appreciate you reading our AI usage guide.

Title: Soporte IT N1 Junior
Description:
Buscamos una persona para incorporarse al equipo de soporte IT en un puesto junior de nivel N1. No buscamos tanto experiencia como actitud. Nos interesa más encontrar a alguien con ganas de aprender, interés real por la informática y motivación para crecer dentro del área de soporte.

La persona que se incorpore participará en la atención a usuarios, gestión de incidencias y tareas básicas del día a día, trabajando junto al resto del equipo y aprendiendo en un entorno real.

Funciones:


atención a usuarios en incidencias del día a día
gestión y seguimiento de tickets
soporte de puesto de trabajo
preparación y configuración de equipos
apoyo en tareas de usuarios, accesos y cambios
escalado al resto del equipo cuando sea necesario


Requisitos


formación profesional en informática, preferiblemente SMR o similar
interés por la informática y el soporte a usuarios
ganas de aprender y desarrollarse profesionalmente
buena actitud, responsabilidad y organización
capacidad para trabajar en equipo
trato correcto con usuarios


Se valorará


conocimientos básicos de Windows, Microsoft 365, hardware o redes
se valorará capacidad para comunicarse y desenvolverse en inglés, tanto por escrito como de forma oral.
cualquier experiencia, aunque sea pequeña, en soporte o atención técnica


Qué ofrecemos


incorporación a un equipo IT interno
aprendizaje y crecimiento dentro del área de soporte
formación práctica en el puesto
participación en el día a día real del servicio


﻿Detalles del empleo


Duración del contrato: inicialmente temporal 3 meses, con objetivo de formación y conversión en indefinido transcurridos 6 meses.
Horario: 8h a 17h / 9h a 19h - viernes intensivo.
Modalidad de trabajo: Hibrido

Title: Data Entry – Mapas Y Base De Datos De Poi (Radares)
Description:
EnRadarbotbuscamos una persona para incorporarse al equipo y ayudar en la actualización y mantenimiento de nuestra base de datos radares y POI de tráfico.

Radarbot es una app de navegación y alertas para conductores utilizada por millones de usuarios en todo el mundo.

La calidad de esta base de datos tiene impacto directo en el producto y en la experiencia del usuario, por lo que buscamos un perfil cuidadoso, constante y con mucha atención al detalle.

Qué harásActualizar y mantener la base de datos radares.

Detectar, revisar e incorporar nueva información siguiendo criterios internos.

Validar y corregir datos para asegurar precisión y consistencia.

Trabajar con mapas, imágenes, coordenadas y referencias geográficas para verificar información.

Revisar incidencias o posibles errores en la base de datos.

Aplicar procesos de revisión de forma rigurosa y ordenada.

Contribuir a mantener un alto nivel de calidad del dato.

Qué buscamosBuen manejo de herramientas informáticas.

Experiencia previa en tareas dedata entry, revisión de datos, back office o funciones similares.

Alta atención al detalle y capacidad para trabajar con precisión.

Perfil organizado, metódico y cómodo con tareas repetitivas.

Capacidad de concentración y constancia en el trabajo diario.

Interés o familiaridad con mapas, geolocalización, cartografía o bases de datos de POI.

Capacidad para seguir criterios y procesos con rigor.

Qué valoraremos especialmenteExperiencia validación de información o control de calidad del dato.

Fluided y velocidad en uso de software informático.

Familiaridad con entornos relacionados con mapas, navegación o datos geolocalizados.

Capacidad para detectar errores pequeños y mantener consistencia en tareas de revisión.

Qué ofrecemosIncorporarte a una app líder a nivel mundial con millones de usuarios.

Formar parte de un equipo cercano, especializado y con foco en calidad.

Contrato indefinido + bonus + seguro médico.

Horario flexible con franja común.

Entorno presencial en Getafe (Madrid) con 1 día de teletrabajo.

Oficinas modernas en Charmex Green Building, Getafe.

Si te gusta el trabajo preciso, ordenado y bien hecho, y te motiva contribuir a la calidad de un producto digital usado por millones de personas, nos encantará conocerte.

Envíanos tu currículum a ****** poniendo en el asunto el puesto al que postulas.

Al inscribirte en esta oferta, consientes el tratamiento de tus datos personales conforme a la normativa vigente en materia de protección de datos (RGPD).
